15	package coralnpu
16	
17	import chisel3._
18	import chisel3.simulator.scalatest.ChiselSim
19	import org.scalatest.freespec.AnyFreeSpec
20	
21	
22	class FetchReorderBufferSpec extends AnyFreeSpec with ChiselSim {
23	  val capacity = 4
24	
25	  def makeDut(flowResponse: Boolean = false) = new FetchReorderBuffer(txidBits=4, addrBits=32, dataBits=128, capacity=capacity, flowResponse=flowResponse)
26	
27	  def runTest(testFn: FetchReorderBuffer => Unit): Unit = {
28	    simulate(makeDut()) { dut =>
29	      dut.io.flush.poke(false.B)
30	      dut.io.newTx.valid.poke(false.B)
31	      dut.io.busResp.valid.poke(false.B)
32	      dut.io.commit.ready.poke(false.B)
33	      testFn(dut)
34	    }
35	  }
36	
37	  def pokeNewTx(dut: => FetchReorderBuffer, txid: UInt, addr: UInt): Unit = {
38	    dut.io.newTx.valid.poke(true.B)
39	    dut.io.newTx.bits.txid.poke(txid)
40	    dut.io.newTx.bits.addr.poke(addr)
41	  }
42	
43	  def enqueueTx(dut: => FetchReorderBuffer, txid: UInt, addr: UInt): Unit = {
44	    pokeNewTx(dut, txid, addr)
45	    expectNewTxReady(dut, true)
46	    dut.clock.step()
47	    dut.io.newTx.valid.poke(false.B)
48	  }
49	
50	  def pokeBusResp(dut: => FetchReorderBuffer, txid: UInt, data: UInt, fault: Bool = false.B): Unit = {
51	    dut.io.busResp.valid.poke(true.B)
52	    dut.io.busResp.bits.txid.poke(txid)
53	    dut.io.busResp.bits.resp.data.poke(data)
54	    dut.io.busResp.bits.resp.fault.poke(fault)
55	  }
56	
57	  def expectFreeTxid(dut: => FetchReorderBuffer, txid: UInt): Unit = {
58	    dut.io.freeTxid.valid.expect(true.B)
59	    dut.io.freeTxid.bits.expect(txid)
60	  }
61	
62	  def expectCommit(dut: => FetchReorderBuffer, addr: UInt, data: UInt, fault: Bool = false.B): Unit = {
63	    dut.io.commit.valid.expect(true.B)
64	    dut.io.commit.bits.addr.expect(addr)
65	    dut.io.commit.bits.resp.data.expect(data)
66	    dut.io.commit.bits.resp.fault.expect(fault)
67	  }
68	
69	  def clearBusResp(dut: => FetchReorderBuffer): Unit = {
70	    dut.io.busResp.valid.poke(false.B)
71	  }
72	
73	  def setCommitReady(dut: => FetchReorderBuffer, ready: Boolean): Unit = {
74	    dut.io.commit.ready.poke(ready.B)
75	  }
76	
77	  def setFlush(dut: => FetchReorderBuffer, flush: Boolean): Unit = {
78	    dut.io.flush.poke(flush.B)
79	  }
80	
81	  def expectNoCommit(dut: => FetchReorderBuffer): Unit = {
82	    dut.io.commit.valid.expect(false.B)
83	  }
84	
85	  def expectNoFreeTxid(dut: => FetchReorderBuffer): Unit = {
86	    dut.io.freeTxid.valid.expect(false.B)
87	  }
88	
89	  def expectNewTxReady(dut: => FetchReorderBuffer, ready: Boolean): Unit = {
90	    dut.io.newTx.ready.expect(ready.B)
91	  }
92	
93	  def setupQueue(dut: => FetchReorderBuffer, txids: Seq[Int], respondedTxids: Seq[Int] = Seq.empty): Unit = {
94	    for (txid <- txids) {
95	      enqueueTx(dut, txid.U, (txid * 0x100).U)
96	    }
97	    for (txid <- respondedTxids) {
98	      pokeBusResp(dut, txid.U, (txid * 0x1000).U)
99	      dut.clock.step()
100	    }
101	    if (respondedTxids.nonEmpty) {
102	      clearBusResp(dut)
103	    }
104	  }
105	
106	  def verifyQueueEmpty(dut: => FetchReorderBuffer): Unit = {
107	    setupQueue(dut, 4 until 4 + capacity)
108	    expectNewTxReady(dut, false)
109	  }
110	
111	  // Note on test coverage:
112	  // Simple base cases like "Unmatched busResp" and "Record busResp" are intentionally omitted
113	  // as standalone tests because they are implicitly and exhaustively covered by the following tests:
114	  // - "Unmatched busResp" logic (freeTxid generation and commit blocking) is fully covered by
115	  //   Condition 3 in the "Commit" test, as well as the "Unmatched busResp and flush" test.
116	  // - "Record busResp" logic is inherently covered by the `setupQueue` helper, which is used
117	  //   across almost all tests, and by the general flow of the "Commit" test.
118	  "Basic Data Flow and Reordering" - {
119	    "Record and Commit with fault" in {
120	      runTest { dut =>
121	        setCommitReady(dut, true)
122	
123	        enqueueTx(dut, 1.U, 0x100.U)
124	        
125	        // Provide response with fault = true
126	        pokeBusResp(dut, 1.U, 0x1000.U, fault = true.B)
127	        dut.clock.step()
128	        clearBusResp(dut)
129	
130	        // Verify the fault bit successfully propagated to the commit stage
131	        expectCommit(dut, 0x100.U, 0x1000.U, fault = true.B)
132	      }
133	    }
134	
135	    "Commit" in {
136	      runTest { dut =>
137	        // Condition 1: Empty buffer blocks commit
138	        expectNoCommit(dut)
139	
140	        enqueueTx(dut, 1.U, 0x100.U)
141	
142	        // Condition 2: No response yet blocks commit
143	        expectNoCommit(dut)
144	
145	        pokeBusResp(dut, 1.U, 0x1000.U)
146	        dut.clock.step()
147	        clearBusResp(dut)
148	
149	        // Commit should now be valid, and commit.ready being low blocks consumption
150	        expectCommit(dut, 0x100.U, 0x1000.U)
151	
152	        // Condition 3: Unmatched busResp takes priority for freeTxid and blocks commit combinatorially
153	        pokeBusResp(dut, 2.U, 0x2000.U)
154	        expectNoCommit(dut)
155	        expectFreeTxid(dut, 2.U)
156	        dut.clock.step()
157	        clearBusResp(dut)
158	
159	        // Back to normal, commit is valid again
160	        expectCommit(dut, 0x100.U, 0x1000.U)
161	
162	        // Consume the first transaction
163	        setCommitReady(dut, true)
164	        dut.clock.step()
165	        setCommitReady(dut, false)
166	        expectNoCommit(dut)
167	
168	        // Setup for Condition 4: Cancelled transaction at the head of the buffer
169	        setupQueue(dut, Seq(2, 3), Seq(2, 3))
170	
171	        // Commit is valid for Tx 2
172	        expectCommit(dut, 0x200.U, 0x2000.U)
173	
174	        // Trigger flush. In this cycle, it's still valid because s2Src=state (from prev cycle)
175	        setFlush(dut, true)
176	        dut.clock.step()
177	        setFlush(dut, false)
178	
179	        // Condition 4: Queue has Tx 3 with valid response, but nCancelled == 1 blocks commit
180	        expectNoCommit(dut)
181	
182	        dut.clock.step()
183	        // Queue is now empty
184	        expectNoCommit(dut)
185	      }
186	    }
187	
188	    "Record and Commit" in {
189	      runTest { dut =>
190	        // Setup: Enqueue Tx 1 and Tx 2
191	        setupQueue(dut, Seq(1, 2), Seq(1))
192	
193	        // Now Tx 1 is ready to commit.
194	        // Simultaneously provide response for Tx 2 and consume Tx 1.
195	        setCommitReady(dut, true)
196	        pokeBusResp(dut, 2.U, 0x2000.U)
197	
198	        // Tx 1 should commit successfully while Tx 2's response is being recorded.
199	        expectCommit(dut, 0x100.U, 0x1000.U)
200	        expectFreeTxid(dut, 1.U)
201	        dut.clock.step()
202	
203	        // In the next cycle, Tx 2 should be at the head and ready to commit,
204	        // proving its response was correctly recorded in the previous cycle.
205	        clearBusResp(dut)
206	        expectCommit(dut, 0x200.U, 0x2000.U)
207	        expectFreeTxid(dut, 2.U)
208	      }
209	    }
210	
211	    "Reorder responses" in {
212	      runTest { dut =>
213	        // Setup: Enqueue Tx 1, 2, 3 and provide response for Tx 3 (out of order)
214	        setupQueue(dut, Seq(1, 2, 3), Seq(3))
215	        
216	        // Tx 1 is at head, un-responded. Commit must be blocked.
217	        expectNoCommit(dut)
218	
219	        // Provide response for Tx 2 (out of order)
220	        pokeBusResp(dut, 2.U, 0x2000.U)
221	        dut.clock.step()
222	
223	        // Commit is still blocked by Tx 1
224	        clearBusResp(dut)
225	        expectNoCommit(dut)
226	
227	        // Provide response for Tx 1 (in order)
228	        pokeBusResp(dut, 1.U, 0x1000.U)
229	        dut.clock.step()
230	        clearBusResp(dut)
231	
232	        // Now Tx 1 is ready! Consume it.
233	        setCommitReady(dut, true)
234	        expectCommit(dut, 0x100.U, 0x1000.U)
235	        expectFreeTxid(dut, 1.U)
236	        dut.clock.step()
237	
238	        // Tx 2 and Tx 3 should immediately follow in perfect order
239	        expectCommit(dut, 0x200.U, 0x2000.U)
240	        expectFreeTxid(dut, 2.U)
241	        dut.clock.step()
242	
243	        expectCommit(dut, 0x300.U, 0x3000.U)
244	        expectFreeTxid(dut, 3.U)
245	        dut.clock.step()
246	
247	        setCommitReady(dut, false)
248	        expectNoCommit(dut)
249	        verifyQueueEmpty(dut)
250	      }
251	    }
252	  }
253	
254	  "Flushing" - {
255	    "Unmatched busResp and flush" in {
256	      runTest { dut =>
257	        // Setup: Enqueue Tx 1 (un-responded), Tx 2 (responded), Tx 3 (un-responded)
258	        setupQueue(dut, Seq(1, 2, 3), Seq(2))
259	
260	        // Trigger flush while simultaneously providing an unmatched busResp
261	        setFlush(dut, true)
262	        pokeBusResp(dut, 4.U, 0x4000.U)
263	
264	        // Unmatched busResp claims freeTxid.
265	        // The flush cancels all transactions. Tx 1 has no response so it is silently dropped.
266	        // Freeing the txid for the cancelled Tx 2 is blocked by the unmatched busResp, so Tx 2 (and Tx 3 shielded behind it) remain in the buffer.
267	        expectFreeTxid(dut, 4.U)
268	        dut.clock.step()
269	
270	        // Remove the unmatched busResp. The buffer frees the txid for Tx 2.
271	        // Tx 3 (cancelled, no response) is silently dropped in the same cycle.
272	        setFlush(dut, false)
273	        clearBusResp(dut)
274	
275	        expectFreeTxid(dut, 2.U)
276	        dut.clock.step()
277	
278	        verifyQueueEmpty(dut)
279	      }
280	    }
281	
282	    "Flush frees txid for responded cancelled transactions" in {
283	      runTest { dut =>
284	        // Setup: Enqueue Tx 1 (un-responded), Tx 2 (responded), Tx 3 (un-responded)
285	        setupQueue(dut, Seq(1, 2, 3), Seq(2))
286	
287	        // Trigger flush with no blocking conditions
288	        setFlush(dut, true)
289	
290	        // In this single cycle:
291	        // 1. Flush cancels all transactions. Tx 1 has no response, so it is silently dropped.
292	        // 2. Tx 2 is cancelled and already has a response, so its txid is freed via freeTxid.
293	        // 3. Tx 3 has no response and is behind Tx 2, so it is silently dropped in the same cascade.
294	        // All transactions are cleared immediately!
295	        expectFreeTxid(dut, 2.U)
296	        dut.clock.step()
297	        setFlush(dut, false)
298	
299	        verifyQueueEmpty(dut)
300	      }
301	    }
302	
303	    "Flush, record busResp, and free cancelled txid" in {
304	      runTest { dut =>
305	        // Setup: Enqueue Tx 1, Tx 2, Tx 3 (all un-responded)
306	        setupQueue(dut, Seq(1, 2, 3))
307	
308	        // Trigger flush while simultaneously providing a matched busResp for Tx 2
309	        setFlush(dut, true)
310	        pokeBusResp(dut, 2.U, 0x2000.U)
311	
312	        // In this single cycle:
313	        // 1. The response for Tx 2 is recorded.
314	        // 2. The flush cancels all transactions. Tx 1 (un-responded) is silently dropped.
315	        // 3. Tx 2 now has a response and is cancelled, so its txid is freed via freeTxid.
316	        // 4. Tx 3 (un-responded) is silently dropped in the same cascade.
317	        expectFreeTxid(dut, 2.U)
318	        expectNoCommit(dut)
319	        dut.clock.step()
320	
321	        setFlush(dut, false)
322	        clearBusResp(dut)
323	
324	        verifyQueueEmpty(dut)
325	      }
326	    }
327	
328	    "Flush, record busResp, and commit" in {
329	      runTest { dut =>
330	        // Setup: Enqueue Tx 1 (responded), Tx 2 (un-responded), Tx 3 (un-responded)
331	        setupQueue(dut, Seq(1, 2, 3), Seq(1))
332	
333	        // Simultaneously:
334	        // 1. Commit Tx 1
335	        // 2. Record response for Tx 2
336	        // 3. Flush
337	        setCommitReady(dut, true)
338	        pokeBusResp(dut, 2.U, 0x2000.U)
339	        setFlush(dut, true)
340	
341	        // Committing Tx 1 claims freeTxid.
342	        // Freeing the txid for the cancelled Tx 2 is blocked by the commit, so Tx 2 is NOT dropped yet.
343	        // Tx 3 remains shielded behind Tx 2.
344	        expectCommit(dut, 0x100.U, 0x1000.U)
345	        expectFreeTxid(dut, 1.U)
346	        dut.clock.step()
347	
348	        // Next cycle: Remove stimuli. The buffer frees the txid for Tx 2, and silently drops Tx 3.
349	        setCommitReady(dut, false)
350	        clearBusResp(dut)
351	        setFlush(dut, false)
352	
353	        expectNoCommit(dut)
354	        expectFreeTxid(dut, 2.U)
355	        dut.clock.step()
356	
357	        verifyQueueEmpty(dut)
358	      }
359	    }
360	
361	    "Record busResp for a cancelled transaction" in {
362	      runTest { dut =>
363	        // Setup: Enqueue Tx 1 (un-responded), Tx 2 (responded), Tx 3 (un-responded)
364	        setupQueue(dut, Seq(1, 2, 3), Seq(2))
365	
366	        // Trigger flush and an unmatched busResp simultaneously to block freeing cancelled txids
367	        setFlush(dut, true)
368	        pokeBusResp(dut, 4.U, 0x4000.U)
369	
370	        // Tx 1 is silently dropped. Tx 2 is cancelled but has a response.
371	        // Freeing Tx 2's txid is blocked by the unmatched busResp (Tx 4), so Tx 2 and Tx 3 stay in the buffer.
372	        expectFreeTxid(dut, 4.U)
373	        expectNoCommit(dut)
374	        dut.clock.step()
375	
376	        // Now Tx 3 is still in the buffer, cancelled, and un-responded.
377	        // Provide its response!
378	        setFlush(dut, false)
379	        pokeBusResp(dut, 3.U, 0x3000.U)
380	
381	        // The buffer records Tx 3's response.
382	        // The buffer frees Tx 2's txid.
383	        // Since Tx 3 now has a response, it is NOT silently dropped and remains in the buffer.
384	        expectFreeTxid(dut, 2.U)
385	        expectNoCommit(dut)
386	        dut.clock.step()
387	
388	        // Now Tx 3 is at the head, cancelled and responded. Its txid is freed.
389	        clearBusResp(dut)
390	        expectFreeTxid(dut, 3.U)
391	        expectNoCommit(dut)
392	        dut.clock.step()
393	
394	        verifyQueueEmpty(dut)
395	      }
396	    }
397	  }
398	
399	  "Queue Full and Combinatorial Unblocking" - {
400	    "Flush and enqueue" in {
401	      runTest { dut =>
402	        // Setup: Queue has Tx 1, Tx 2
403	        setupQueue(dut, Seq(1, 2))
404	
405	        // Same cycle: flush the queue AND enqueue Tx 3
406	        setFlush(dut, true)
407	        pokeNewTx(dut, 3.U, 0x300.U)
408	        expectNewTxReady(dut, true)
409	        dut.clock.step()
410	
411	        setFlush(dut, false)
412	        dut.io.newTx.valid.poke(false.B)
413	        dut.clock.step()
414	
415	        // Tx 1 and Tx 2 are cancelled by the flush and have no responses, so they are
416	        // silently dropped from the buffer.
417	        // Tx 3 is added AFTER the flush marker in the combinatorial chain, so it is NOT cancelled.
418	        // Tx 3 is now at the head, waiting for response.
419	
420	        // Provide response for Tx 3
421	        pokeBusResp(dut, 3.U, 0x3000.U)
422	        dut.clock.step()
423	        clearBusResp(dut)
424	
425	        // Verify Tx 3 commits successfully (proving it wasn't cancelled)
426	        expectCommit(dut, 0x300.U, 0x3000.U)
427	      }
428	    }
429	
430	    "Commit and enqueue" in {
431	      runTest { dut =>
432	        setupQueue(dut, 1 to capacity, Seq(1))
433	        expectNewTxReady(dut, false)
434	
435	        setCommitReady(dut, true)
436	        pokeNewTx(dut, 5.U, 0x500.U)
437	        
438	        expectCommit(dut, 0x100.U, 0x1000.U)
439	        expectNewTxReady(dut, true)
440	        dut.clock.step()
441	
442	        setCommitReady(dut, false)
443	        dut.io.newTx.valid.poke(false.B)
444	        
445	        // Buffer is full again (Tx 2, 3, 4, 5 waiting for responses)
446	        expectNewTxReady(dut, false)
447	      }
448	    }
449	
450	    "Drop cancelled transaction and enqueue" in {
451	      runTest { dut =>
452	        setupQueue(dut, 1 to capacity)
453	        expectNewTxReady(dut, false)
454	
455	        setFlush(dut, true)
456	        pokeBusResp(dut, 1.U, 0x1000.U)
457	        
458	        expectFreeTxid(dut, 1.U)
459	        
460	        pokeNewTx(dut, 5.U, 0x500.U)
461	        expectNewTxReady(dut, true)
462	        dut.clock.step()
463	      }
464	    }
465	  }
466	
467	  "flowResponse=true" - {
468	    "Same-cycle commit" in {
469	      // Note: We call simulate directly because the `runTest` wrapper defaults to flowResponse=false
470	      simulate(makeDut(flowResponse = true)) { d =>
471	        d.io.flush.poke(false.B)
472	        d.io.newTx.valid.poke(false.B)
473	        d.io.busResp.valid.poke(false.B)
474	        d.io.commit.ready.poke(false.B)
475	
476	        // Enqueue Tx 1
477	        enqueueTx(d, 1.U, 0x100.U)
478	
479	        // Currently at head, waiting for response. Commit must be blocked.
480	        expectNoCommit(d)
481	
482	        // Provide response. Because flowResponse=true, commit goes valid immediately!
483	        pokeBusResp(d, 1.U, 0x1000.U)
484	        
485	        // Unblocked in the SAME cycle
486	        expectCommit(d, 0x100.U, 0x1000.U)
487	
488	        // Consume it immediately
489	        setCommitReady(d, true)
490	        d.clock.step()
491	
492	        // Verify consumption
493	        clearBusResp(d)
494	        setCommitReady(d, false)
495	        expectNoCommit(d)
496	        expectNewTxReady(d, true)
497	        
498	        // Enqueue enough to prove the buffer is empty
499	        verifyQueueEmpty(d)
500	      }
501	    }
502	
503	    "Same-cycle commit with fault" in {
504	      simulate(makeDut(flowResponse = true)) { d =>
505	        d.io.flush.poke(false.B)
506	        d.io.newTx.valid.poke(false.B)
507	        d.io.busResp.valid.poke(false.B)
508	        d.io.commit.ready.poke(false.B)
509	
510	        // Enqueue Tx 1
511	        enqueueTx(d, 1.U, 0x100.U)
512	
513	        // Currently at head, waiting for response. Commit must be blocked.
514	        expectNoCommit(d)
515	
516	        // Provide response with fault = true. Because flowResponse=true, commit goes valid immediately!
517	        pokeBusResp(d, 1.U, 0x1000.U, fault = true.B)
518	        
519	        // Unblocked in the SAME cycle, and the fault bit is propagated!
520	        expectCommit(d, 0x100.U, 0x1000.U, fault = true.B)
521	
522	        // Consume it immediately
523	        setCommitReady(d, true)
524	        d.clock.step()
525	
526	        // Verify consumption
527	        clearBusResp(d)
528	        setCommitReady(d, false)
529	        expectNoCommit(d)
530	        expectNewTxReady(d, true)
531	        
532	        verifyQueueEmpty(d)
533	      }
534	    }
535	  }
536	}

21	class PredecodeOutput(p: Parameters) extends Bundle {
22	    val insts = Vec(p.fetchInstrSlots, new FetchInstruction(p))
23	    val count = UInt(4.W)
24	    val nextPc = UInt(p.instructionBits.W)
25	    val hasJumped = Bool()
26	}
27	
28	class FetchResponse(p: Parameters) extends Bundle {
29	    val addr = UInt(p.fetchAddrBits.W)
30	    val inst = Vec(p.fetchInstrSlots, UInt(p.instructionBits.W))
31	    val fault = Bool()
32	}
33	
34	class Instruction(p: Parameters) extends Bundle {
35	    val addr = UInt(p.fetchAddrBits.W)
36	    val inst = UInt(p.instructionBits.W)
37	}
38	
39	class FetchReorderBuffer(txidBits: Int, addrBits: Int, dataBits: Int, capacity: Int, flowResponse: Boolean = false) extends Module {
40	  val ctrWidth = log2Ceil(capacity + 1)
41	  val indexWidth = log2Ceil(capacity)
42	
43	  // Structures
44	
45	  class Response extends Bundle {
46	    val data = UInt(dataBits.W)
47	    val fault = Bool()
48	  }
49	
50	  class NewTx extends Bundle {
51	    val txid = UInt(txidBits.W)
52	    val addr = UInt(addrBits.W)
53	  }
54	
55	  // This comes from the bus
56	  class ResponseWithTxid extends Bundle {
57	    val txid = UInt(txidBits.W)
58	    val resp = new Response()
59	  }
60	
61	  // This is just FetchResponse before instructions are separated.
62	  // I don't want the reorder buffer to understand the idea of instructions.
63	  class ResponseWithAddr extends Bundle {
64	    val addr = UInt(addrBits.W)
65	    val resp = new Response()
66	  }
67	
68	  class Entry extends Bundle {
69	    val txid = UInt(txidBits.W)
70	    val addr = UInt(addrBits.W)
71	    val resp = Valid(new Response())
72	
73	    def asResponse: ResponseWithAddr = MakeWireBundle[ResponseWithAddr](
74	        new ResponseWithAddr,
75	        _.addr -> addr,
76	        _.resp -> resp.bits,
77	    )
78	  }
79	  object Entry {
80	    def apply(): Entry = MakeWireBundle[Entry](
81	      new Entry,
82	      _.txid -> 0.U,
83	      _.addr -> 0.U,
84	      _.resp -> MakeInvalid(new Response),
85	    )
86	  }
87	
88	  // IO
89	
90	  val io = IO(new Bundle {
91	    val newTx = Flipped(Decoupled(new NewTx()))
92	    // We always consume the resp. It is discarded if nothing matches.
93	    val busResp = Flipped(Valid(new ResponseWithTxid()))
94	    val commit = Decoupled(new ResponseWithAddr())
95	    // Allocator should always have space for what it allocated.
96	    val freeTxid = Valid(UInt(txidBits.W))
97	    val flush = Input(Bool())
98	  })
99	
100	  class State extends Bundle {
101	    val queue = Vec(capacity, new Entry)
102	    val nElem = UInt(ctrWidth.W)
103	    val nCancelled = UInt(ctrWidth.W)
104	
105	    def valids = VecInit.tabulate(capacity) (_.U < nElem)
106	
107	    def trimPrecondition = {
108	      val noOverflow = nElem <= capacity.U
109	      val nCancelledReasonable = nCancelled <= nElem
110	      val noDuplicateTxid = !VecInit.tabulate(capacity, capacity) { (i, j) =>
111	        (i != j).B && state.valids(i) && state.valids(j) && state.queue(i).txid === state.queue(j).txid
112	      }.exists(x => x.exists(y => y))
113	
114	      noOverflow && nCancelledReasonable && noDuplicateTxid
115	    }
116	
117	    def commonPrecondition = {
118	      val dontNeedTrim = nCancelled === 0.U || queue(0).resp.valid
119	
120	      trimPrecondition && dontNeedTrim
121	    }
122	
123	    def trySaveResponse(busResp: ResponseWithTxid): (State, Bool) = {
124	      val founds = VecInit.tabulate(capacity) {i =>
125	        valids(i) && (busResp.txid === queue(i).txid)
126	      }
127	      // Resp cannot match two tx.
128	      val duplicateTxid = founds.count(x => x) > 1.U
129	      // Tx cannot get two resp.
130	      val badResponse = VecInit.tabulate(capacity) {i =>
131	        founds(i) && queue(i).resp.valid
132	      }.exists(x => x)
133	      val precondition = commonPrecondition && !duplicateTxid && !badResponse
134	
135	      val accepted = Mux(precondition, founds.exists(x => x), WireDefault(Bool(), DontCare))
136	      val result = Mux(precondition, Mux(accepted, MakeWireBundle[State](
137	          new State,
138	          _ -> this,
139	          _.queue(founds.indexWhere(x => x)).resp -> MakeValid(busResp.resp),
140	      ), this), State.unreachable())
141	
142	      (result, accepted)
143	    }
144	
145	    def enq(tx: NewTx): State = {
146	      val precondition = commonPrecondition && nElem < capacity.U
147	
148	      val index = nElem(indexWidth - 1, 0)
149	      Mux(precondition, MakeWireBundle[State](
150	          new State,
151	          _ -> this,
152	          _.queue(index).txid -> tx.txid,
153	          _.queue(index).addr -> tx.addr,
154	          _.queue(index).resp.valid -> false.B,
155	          // _.queue(index).resp.bits is untouched
156	          _.nElem -> (nElem + 1.U),
157	      ), State.unreachable())
158	    }
159	
160	    def deq(): State = {
161	      val precondition = commonPrecondition && nElem > 0.U
162	
163	      Mux(precondition, MakeWireBundle[State](
164	          new State,
165	          _ -> this,
166	          _.queue -> VecInit.tabulate(capacity) {i =>
167	            if (i + 1 < capacity)
168	              Mux((i + 1).U < nElem, queue(i + 1), queue(i))
169	            else queue(i)
170	          },
171	          _.nElem -> (nElem - 1.U),
172	          _.nCancelled -> Mux(nCancelled > 0.U, nCancelled - 1.U, 0.U),
173	      ), State.unreachable())
174	    }
175	
176	    def flush(): State = {
177	      Mux(commonPrecondition, MakeWireBundle[State](
178	          new State,
179	          _ -> this,
180	          _.nCancelled -> nElem,
181	      ), State.unreachable())
182	    }
183	
184	    def maybeTrim(): State = {
185	      // From the front, discard all cancelled entries without resp
186	      val keepCancelled = VecInit.tabulate(capacity) {i =>
187	        (i.U < nCancelled) && queue(i).resp.valid
188	      }
189	      val hasKeep = keepCancelled.exists(x => x)
190	      val offset = Mux(hasKeep, keepCancelled.indexWhere(x => x), nCancelled)
191	
192	      // Note it's not commonPrecondition here
193	      Mux(trimPrecondition, MakeWireBundle[State](
194	          new State,
195	          _ -> this,
196	          _.queue -> VecInit.tabulate(capacity) {i =>
197	            Mux(i.U < nElem - offset, queue(i.U + offset(indexWidth - 1, 0)), queue(i))
198	          },
199	          _.nElem -> (nElem - offset),
200	          _.nCancelled -> (nCancelled - offset),
201	      ), State.unreachable())
202	    }
203	  }
204	  object State {
205	    def apply() = MakeWireBundle[State](
206	        new State,
207	        _.queue -> VecInit.fill(capacity)(Entry()),
208	        _.nElem -> 0.U,
209	        _.nCancelled -> 0.U,
210	    )
211	
212	    def unreachable() = MakeWireBundle[State](
213	        new State,
214	        _.queue -> DontCare,
215	        _.nElem -> DontCare,
216	        _.nCancelled -> DontCare,
217	    )
218	  }
219	
220	  val state = RegInit(State())
221	
222	  // Stage 1, decide what to do with the bus response
223	  val badResponse = io.busResp.valid && VecInit.tabulate(capacity) {i =>
224	    state.valids(i) && state.queue(i).resp.valid && state.queue(i).txid === io.busResp.bits.txid
225	  }.exists(x => x)
226	  val s1Precondition = state.commonPrecondition && !badResponse
227	  assert(s1Precondition)
228	  val (stateAfterResp, respFound) = state.trySaveResponse(io.busResp.bits)
229	  // BusResp takes priority here because s1Reject cannot depend on io.flush.
230	  val s1Reject = io.busResp.valid && !respFound
231	  val s1State = Mux(s1Precondition, Mux(io.busResp.valid, stateAfterResp, state), State.unreachable())
232	
233	  // Stage 2, commit a response if we can
234	  val s2Src = if (flowResponse) s1State else state
235	  // Prevent io.commit.valid from depending on io.commit.valid
236	  val s2Valid = !s1Reject && s2Src.valids(0) && s2Src.nCancelled === 0.U && s2Src.queue(0).resp.valid
237	  val s2Commit = io.commit.fire
238	  val s2Precondition = s2Src.commonPrecondition && (!s2Commit || s2Valid)
239	  assert(s2Precondition)
240	  val s2State = Mux(s2Precondition, Mux(s2Commit, s1State.deq(), s1State), State.unreachable())
241	
242	  // Stage 3, handle flushing
243	  val s3Precondition = s2State.commonPrecondition
244	  assert(s3Precondition)
245	  val s3State = Mux(s3Precondition, Mux(io.flush, s2State.flush().maybeTrim(), s2State), State.unreachable())
246	
247	  // Stage 4, discard a response if we can
248	  val s4Discard = !s1Reject && !s2Commit && s3State.valids(0) && s3State.nCancelled > 0.U
249	  val s4Precondition = s3State.commonPrecondition && (!s4Discard || s3State.queue(0).resp.valid)
250	  assert(s4Precondition)
251	  val s4State = Mux(s4Precondition, Mux(s4Discard, s3State.deq().maybeTrim(), s3State), State.unreachable())
252	
253	  // Stage 5, handle new transaction
254	  val s5Ready = !s4State.valids(capacity - 1)
255	  val s5Fire = io.newTx.fire
256	  val s5Precondition = s4State.commonPrecondition && (!s5Fire || s5Ready)
257	  assert(s5Precondition)
258	  val s5State = Mux(s5Precondition, Mux(s5Fire, s4State.enq(io.newTx.bits), s4State), State.unreachable())
259	
260	  assert(s5State.commonPrecondition)
261	  state := s5State
262	
263	  io.newTx.ready := s5Ready
264	  io.commit.valid := s2Valid
265	  io.commit.bits := s2Src.queue(0).asResponse
266	  val freeTxidUsage = VecInit(Seq(s1Reject, s2Commit, s4Discard)).count(x => x)
267	  assert(freeTxidUsage <= 1.U)
268	  io.freeTxid := MuxUpTo1H(MakeInvalid(0.U(txidBits.W)), Seq(
269	      s1Reject -> MakeValid(io.busResp.bits.txid),
270	      s2Commit -> MakeValid(s2Src.queue(0).txid),
271	      s4Discard -> MakeValid(s3State.queue(0).txid),
272	  ))
273	}

275	// TODO(atv): Privatize this and FetchControl
276	// Module which is responsible for performing
277	// memory fetches which are requested by
278	// `FetchControl`.
279	class Fetcher(p: Parameters) extends Module {
280	  val io = IO(new Bundle {
281	    val ctrl = Flipped(Irrevocable(UInt(p.fetchAddrBits.W)))
282	    val flushTx = Input(Bool())
283	    val fetch = Decoupled(new FetchResponse(p))
284	    val ibus = new IBusIO(p)
285	  })
286	
287	  val lsb = log2Ceil(p.fetchDataBits / 8)
288	  assert((p.fetchDataBits == 128 && lsb == 4) || (p.fetchDataBits == 256 && lsb == 5))
289	
290	  val maxConcurrentTx = 2
291	  val txidAllocator = Module(new IndexAllocatorShifting(maxConcurrentTx))
292	
293	  // The reorder buffer does not flow the response. This serves as the delay
294	  // cycle to break the rdata->addr loop.
295	  // TODO(davidgao): upgrade ibus and move the delay upstream.
296	  val reorderBuffer = Module(new FetchReorderBuffer(
297	      txidBits=txidAllocator.width,
298	      addrBits=p.fetchAddrBits,
299	      dataBits=p.fetchDataBits,
300	      capacity=maxConcurrentTx,
301	      flowResponse=false,
302	  ))
303	
304	  val canStartFetch = io.ctrl.valid && reorderBuffer.io.newTx.ready && txidAllocator.io.alloc.valid
305	  // The fetch request goes through without stopping.
306	  io.ibus.valid := canStartFetch
307	  io.ibus.addr := Cat(io.ctrl.bits(p.fetchAddrBits - 1, lsb), 0.U(lsb.W))
308	
309	  val ibusAddrFire = io.ibus.fire
310	  // TODO(davidgao): Add txid to ibus interface
311	  // io.ibus.txid := txidAllocator.io.alloc.bits
312	  txidAllocator.io.alloc.ready := ibusAddrFire
313	  reorderBuffer.io.newTx.valid := ibusAddrFire
314	  reorderBuffer.io.newTx.bits.addr := io.ctrl.bits
315	  reorderBuffer.io.newTx.bits.txid := txidAllocator.io.alloc.bits
316	  // TODO(davidgao): remove this adapter when we decouple data from addr on ibus
317	  reorderBuffer.io.busResp.valid := RegNext(ibusAddrFire, false.B)
318	  reorderBuffer.io.busResp.bits.txid := RegNext(txidAllocator.io.alloc.bits)
319	  reorderBuffer.io.busResp.bits.resp.data := io.ibus.rdata
320	  reorderBuffer.io.busResp.bits.resp.fault := RegNext(io.ibus.fault.valid)
321	  reorderBuffer.io.commit.ready := io.fetch.ready
322	  reorderBuffer.io.flush := io.flushTx
323	  io.ctrl.ready := ibusAddrFire
324	
325	  txidAllocator.io.free.valid := reorderBuffer.io.freeTxid.valid
326	  txidAllocator.io.free.bits := reorderBuffer.io.freeTxid.bits
327	
328	  io.fetch.valid := reorderBuffer.io.commit.valid
329	  io.fetch.bits := MakeWireBundle[FetchResponse](
330	      new FetchResponse(p),
331	      _.addr -> reorderBuffer.io.commit.bits.addr,
332	      _.inst -> UIntToVec(reorderBuffer.io.commit.bits.resp.data, p.instructionBits),
333	      _.fault -> reorderBuffer.io.commit.bits.resp.fault,
334	  )
335	}
336	
337	class FetchControl(p: Parameters) extends Module {
338	    val io = IO(new Bundle {
339	        val fetchFault = Valid(UInt(p.programCounterBits.W))
340	        val csr = new CsrInIO(p)
341	        val iflush = Input(Valid(UInt(p.programCounterBits.W)))
342	        val branch = Input(Valid(UInt(p.fetchAddrBits.W)))
343	        val fetchData = Flipped(Decoupled(new FetchResponse(p)))
344	        val linkPort = Flipped(new RegfileLinkPortIO(p))
345	
346	        val fetchAddr = Irrevocable(UInt(p.fetchAddrBits.W))
347	        val flushTx = Output(Bool())
348	        val bufferRequest = DecoupledVectorIO(new FetchInstruction(p), p.fetchInstrSlots)
349	    })
350	
351	    val lsb = log2Ceil(p.fetchDataBits / 8)
352	
353	    def PredictJump(addr: UInt, inst: UInt): ValidIO[UInt] = {
354	      assert(p.instructionBits == 32)
355	      val jal = inst === BitPat("b????????????????????_?????_1101111")
356	      val immjal = Cat(Fill(12, inst(31)), inst(19,12), inst(20), inst(30,21), 0.U(1.W))
357	      val bxx = inst === BitPat("b???????_?????_?????_???_?????_1100011") &&
358	                  inst(31) && inst(14,13) =/= 1.U
359	      val immbxx = Cat(Fill(20, inst(31)), inst(7), inst(30,25), inst(11,8), 0.U(1.W))
360	      val immed = Mux(inst(2), immjal, immbxx)
361	
362	      val valid = jal || bxx
363	      val target = addr + immed
364	
365	      MakeValid(valid, target)
366	    }
367	
368	    def Predecode(fetchResponse: FetchResponse): PredecodeOutput = {
369	      val addr = fetchResponse.addr
370	      val lsb = log2Ceil(p.fetchDataBits / 8)
371	      assert((p.fetchDataBits == 128 && lsb == 4) || (p.fetchDataBits == 256 && lsb == 5))
372	      val baseAddr = addr(p.fetchAddrBits - 1, lsb)
373	      val startElem = addr(lsb - 1, lsb - log2Ceil(p.fetchInstrSlots))
374	
375	      val insts = ShiftVectorRight(fetchResponse.inst, startElem)
376	      val addrs = VecInit.tabulate(p.fetchInstrSlots)(i =>
377	          addr + (i * 4).U
378	      )
379	
380	      val branchTargets = VecInit.tabulate(p.fetchInstrSlots)(i =>
381	          PredictJump(addrs(i), insts(i))
382	      )
383	
384	      val validsIn = VecInit.tabulate(p.fetchInstrSlots)(i =>
385	          i.U < p.fetchInstrSlots.U - startElem
386	      )
387	      val jumped = VecInit.tabulate(p.fetchInstrSlots)(i =>
388	          validsIn(i) && branchTargets(i).valid
389	      )
390	      val firstJumpOH = VecInit(PriorityEncoderOH(jumped))
391	
392	      // Have we jumped before the instruction i
393	      val hasJumpedBefore = VecInit(jumped.scan(false.B)(_||_).take(p.fetchInstrSlots))
394	
395	      val validsOut = VecInit.tabulate(p.fetchInstrSlots)(i =>
396	          validsIn(i) && !hasJumpedBefore(i)
397	      )
398	
399	      val nextFetchPc = MuxUpTo1H(Cat(baseAddr + 1.U, 0.U(lsb.W)),
400	          (0 until p.fetchInstrSlots).map(i => firstJumpOH(i) -> branchTargets(i).bits))
401	
402	      val result = MakeWireBundle[PredecodeOutput](
403	          new PredecodeOutput(p),
404	          _.insts -> VecInit.tabulate(p.fetchInstrSlots)(i =>
405	              MakeWireBundle[FetchInstruction](
406	                  new FetchInstruction(p),
407	                  _.addr -> addrs(i),
408	                  _.inst -> insts(i),
409	                  _.brchFwd -> jumped(i),
410	              )
411	          ),
412	          _.count -> validsOut.count(x => x),
413	          _.nextPc -> nextFetchPc,
414	          _.hasJumped -> jumped.reduce(_||_),
415	      )
416	
417	      result
418	    }
419	
420	    val predecode = Predecode(io.fetchData.bits)
421	
422	    io.bufferRequest.bits := predecode.insts
423	
424	    val pastBranchOrFlush = RegInit(false.B)
425	    val currentBranchOrFlush = io.iflush.valid || io.branch.valid
426	    val ongoingBranchOrFlush = pastBranchOrFlush || currentBranchOrFlush
427	
428	    // If we have faulted we should stop making any new attempts until a branch resolves it.
429	    val faulted = RegInit(false.B)
430	    val fetchFaultValid = (faulted || (io.fetchData.valid && io.fetchData.bits.fault)) &&
431	        !io.branch.valid
432	    io.fetchFault := MakeValid(fetchFaultValid, io.fetchData.bits.addr)
433	    faulted := fetchFaultValid
434	
435	    val sufficientBuffer = io.bufferRequest.nReady >= predecode.count
436	    io.fetchData.ready := sufficientBuffer || fetchFaultValid
437	    // Send out results. All branch or flush, current or past, will make us
438	    // discard results.
439	    // TODO(davidgao): ForceZero it when invalid?
440	    val writeToBuffer = io.fetchData.fire && !fetchFaultValid && !ongoingBranchOrFlush
441	    val nValid = Mux(writeToBuffer, predecode.count, 0.U)
442	    io.bufferRequest.nValid := nValid
443	
444	    val ongoingFetch = RegInit(MakeInvalid(UInt(p.fetchAddrBits.W)))
445	
446	    // PC is initialized with the CSR value below upon leaving reset.
447	    val pc = RegInit(MakeInvalid(UInt(32.W)))
448	
449	    // Past branch or flush doesn't block us from initiating new fetches.
450	    val blockNewFetch = !pc.valid ||  // We're stil in reset.
451	                        currentBranchOrFlush ||
452	                        ongoingFetch.valid ||
453	                        fetchFaultValid
454	
455	    val pcFetched = RegInit(false.B)
456	    pcFetched := MuxCase(pcFetched, Seq(
457	        (!pc.valid) -> !blockNewFetch,  // We're leaving reset.
458	        (io.iflush.valid || io.branch.valid) -> false.B,
459	        (writeToBuffer && predecode.hasJumped) -> !blockNewFetch,
460	        // Speculative fetch
461	        (!blockNewFetch) -> true.B,
462	    ))
463	    val pcNext = MuxCase(pc.bits, Seq(
464	        (!pc.valid) -> Cat(io.csr.value(0)(31,2), 0.U(2.W)),  // We're leaving reset.
465	        io.iflush.valid -> io.iflush.bits,
466	        io.branch.valid -> io.branch.bits,
467	        (writeToBuffer && predecode.hasJumped) -> predecode.nextPc,
468	        // Speculative fetch
469	        (!blockNewFetch && pcFetched) -> Cat(pc.bits(31, lsb) + 1.U, 0.U(lsb.W)),
470	    ))
471	    // PC will always be valid as soon as we leave reset.
472	    pc := MakeValid(pcNext)
473	
474	    val fetch = MuxUpTo1H(MakeInvalid(UInt(p.fetchAddrBits.W)), Seq(
475	        ongoingFetch.valid -> ongoingFetch,
476	        !blockNewFetch -> MakeValid(pcNext),
477	    ))
478	    ongoingFetch := Mux(io.fetchAddr.ready, MakeInvalid(UInt(p.fetchAddrBits.W)), fetch)
479	
480	    // All branch or flush are cleared once we're able to initiate a new fetch.
481	    val newFetchInitiated = fetch.valid && !ongoingFetch.valid
482	    pastBranchOrFlush := ongoingBranchOrFlush && !newFetchInitiated
483	
484	    // Similarly, whenever we write a fetched jump, we need to flush until we initiate a new fetch
485	    val newJump = writeToBuffer && predecode.hasJumped
486	    val pendingJump = RegInit(false.B)
487	    pendingJump := (pendingJump || newJump) && !newFetchInitiated
488	
489	    io.fetchAddr <> MakeIrrevocable(fetch)
490	    io.flushTx := ongoingBranchOrFlush || pendingJump || newJump
491	}
492	
493	class UncachedFetch(p: Parameters) extends FetchUnit(p) {
494	  // TODO(derekjchow): Make Bru use valid interface
495	  val branch = MuxCase(
496	      MakeInvalid(UInt(p.fetchAddrBits.W)),
497	      (0 until p.instructionLanes).map(i =>
498	          io.branch(i).valid -> MakeValid(io.branch(i).value)
499	      ))
500	
501	  val ctrl = Module(new FetchControl(p))
502	  ctrl.io.csr <> io.csr
503	  ctrl.io.branch := branch
504	  val debug_iflush = Seq(
505	    io.debug_pc.valid -> MakeValid(io.debug_pc.bits),
506	  )
507	  ctrl.io.iflush := MuxCase(MakeInvalid(UInt(p.fetchAddrBits.W)), Seq(
508	    io.iflush.valid -> MakeValid(io.iflush.pcNext),
509	  ) ++ debug_iflush)
510	  ctrl.io.linkPort := io.linkPort
511	  // TODO(derekjchow): Maybe do something with back pressure?
512	  io.iflush.ready := true.B
513	
514	  val fetcher = Module(new Fetcher(p))
515	  fetcher.io.ctrl <> ctrl.io.fetchAddr
516	  fetcher.io.flushTx := ctrl.io.flushTx
517	  ctrl.io.fetchData <> fetcher.io.fetch
518	  fetcher.io.ibus <> io.ibus
519	
520	  val window = p.fetchInstrSlots * 2
521	  val instructionBuffer = Module(new InstructionBuffer(
522	      new FetchInstruction(p), p.fetchInstrSlots, window))
523	  instructionBuffer.io.feedIn <> ctrl.io.bufferRequest
524	  io.inst.lanes <> instructionBuffer.io.out.take(4)
525	  instructionBuffer.io.flush := io.iflush.valid || branch.valid || io.debug_pc.valid
526

20	class CircularBufferMulti[T <: Data](t: T, n: Int, capacity: Int) extends Module {
21	  // For the time being, restrict to powers of 2
22	  assert(isPow2(n))
23	  assert(isPow2(capacity))
24	  val io = IO(new Bundle {
25	    val enqValid = Input(UInt(log2Ceil(n + 1).W))
26	    val enqData = Input(Vec(n, t))
27	
28	    val nEnqueued = Output(UInt(log2Ceil(capacity + 1).W))
29	    val nSpace = Output(UInt(log2Ceil(capacity + 1).W))
30	
31	    val dataOut = Output(Vec(n, t))
32	    val deqReady = Input(UInt(log2Ceil(n + 1).W))
33	
34	    val flush = Input(Bool())
35	  })
36	  dontTouch(io)
37	
38	  // Note first assert below should be sufficient it allows enqueueing items when buffer
39	  // is full or close to full provided deqReady >= enqValid.
40	  // The second assert is more conservative, and will never allow enqueueing more items
41	  // than there is space in the buffer, under any circumstances. May be removed if
42	  // desire for more is greater than the need to be more conservative.
43	  assert(io.nEnqueued +& io.enqValid -& io.deqReady <= capacity.U)
44	  assert(io.enqValid <= (capacity.U -& io.nEnqueued))
45	
46	  assert(io.deqReady <= io.nEnqueued)
47	
48	  val buffer = RegInit(VecInit.fill(capacity)(0.U.asTypeOf(t)))
49	  val enqPtr = RegInit(0.U(log2Ceil(capacity).W))
50	  val deqPtr = RegInit(0.U(log2Ceil(capacity).W))
51	
52	  val expandedInput = Wire(Vec(capacity, Valid(t)))
53	  for (i <- 0 until capacity) {
54	    if (i < n) {
55	      expandedInput(i) := MakeValid(i.U < io.enqValid, io.enqData(i))
56	    } else {
57	      expandedInput(i) := MakeInvalid(t)
58	    }
59	  }
60	
61	  val rotatedInput = RotateVectorLeft(expandedInput, enqPtr)
62	  for (i <- 0 until capacity) {
63	    buffer(i) := Mux(rotatedInput(i).valid, rotatedInput(i).bits, buffer(i))
64	  }
65	
66	  var nEnqueued = RegInit(0.U(io.nEnqueued.getWidth.W))
67	  enqPtr    := Mux(io.flush, 0.U, enqPtr + io.enqValid)
68	  deqPtr    := Mux(io.flush, 0.U, deqPtr + io.deqReady)
69	  nEnqueued := Mux(io.flush, 0.U, nEnqueued + io.enqValid - io.deqReady)
70	
71	  io.nEnqueued := nEnqueued
72	  io.nSpace := capacity.U - nEnqueued
73	
74	  val outputBufferView = RotateVectorRight(buffer, deqPtr)
75	  for (i <- 0 until n) {
76	    io.dataOut(i) := outputBufferView(i)
77	  }
78	}

81	  // enables the full Retirement Buffer (ROB) tracking mode (mini = false).
82	  // Under configurations with RVV disabled but Float enabled (like core_mini_axi_sim),
83	  // the full ROB mode has hardware bugs/hangs (e.g. vector store waits and reset
84	  // oscillations). We still need the debug ports exposed for these targets to
85	  // generate instruction traces for riscv-dv co-simulation, but we must run
86	  // the ROB in mini mode (enableVerification = false) to avoid the hangs.

content.thinking:
Now I have the Parameters context. Key facts:
- `instructionBits = 32`
- `instructionLanes = 4` (this is the number of decoders, i.e., 4-issue)
- `fetchDataBits = 256` by default (256 bits = 8 instructions per fetch line); can be 128 in SoC config
- `fetchInstrSlots = fetchDataBits / instructionBits = 256/32 = 8` (8 instructions per fetch)
- `fetchAddrBits = 32`
- `fetchCacheBytes = 1024` (1KB L0 cache)
- `enableFetchL0 = true` (L0 cache enabled by default)

Now, let me understand the two fetch paths:
1. `Fetch` (Fetch.scala) — "Instruction fetch unit, with an integrated L0 cache" — this is used when `enableFetchL0 = true`. It's a 4-way fetcher with integrated L0 cache and inline predecode.
2. `UncachedFetch` (UncachedFetch.scala) — uses `FetchControl` + `Fetcher` + `FetchReorderBuffer` + `InstructionBuffer`. This is the newer "uncached" path (without the L0 cache, uses a small reorder buffer for 2 outstanding fetches).

Wait, let me check which one is actually used. Let me look at how the Core instantiates the fetch unit. Let me check the Core to see whether it uses Fetch or UncachedFetch based on enableFetchL0.

Actually, the key for the lecture is to explain the concepts. Let me look at how these are wired. Let me search for where Fetch vs UncachedFetch is instantiated.

Actually, I have enough understanding now. Let me reconsider the focus of this lecture per the spec:
- Topic: 讲解 Fetch/FetchControl/FetchReorderBuffer/InstructionBuffer 如何实现多发射取指、分支预测策略与指令缓冲。
- Learning goals: 
  1. 理解四发射取指的宽通道与 InstructionBuffer 缓冲机制 (understand 4-issue fetch wide channel & InstructionBuffer buffering)
  2. 掌握向后跳 taken、向前跳 not-taken 的分支预测策略及惩罚 (backward-taken / forward-not-taken prediction and penalty)
  3. 了解 FetchReorderBuffer 在取指乱序时的重排序作用 (FetchReorderBuffer's role in reordering out-of-order fetch responses)

The minimal modules:
- Fetch.scala
- FetchControlSpec.scala
- InstructionBuffer.scala

So I'll structure the lecture around:
1. 4-issue wide fetch + the wide channel (256-bit = 8 instructions, but 4 decoders/instructionLanes)
2. InstructionBuffer as the elastic buffer between fetch and dispatch
3. Branch prediction (backward taken / forward not-taken) — this is in FetchControl's PredictJump + Fetch's PredecodeDe
4. FetchReorderBuffer's reorder role

Wait, I need to clarify the relationship. Let me re-examine.

There are two fetch implementations:
1. `Fetch` (Fetch.scala) — L0 cache based, used when `enableFetchL0`. Branch prediction "backwards branches are assumed taken and forward branches assumed not taken" (header comment lines 16-18). It has `Predecode` (only JAL) and `PredecodeDe` (jal, ret, bxx where op(31) set = backward branch taken). Let me look more closely.

In `Fetch.scala`:
- `Predecode` (line 109): only detects JAL (opcode 1101111).
- `PredecodeDe` (line 325): detects:
  - `jal`: opcode 1101111 (JAL — unconditional jump, always taken)
  - `ret`: JALR to ra (x1) when linkPort valid — return prediction
  - `bxx`: branch (opcode 1100011) AND `op(31)` (the sign bit of B-type immediate, which is 1 when the branch target is backward) AND `op(14,13) =/= 1` (not the reserved encoding). 

So `op(31)` is the sign bit of the B-type immediate. If op(31)=1, the immediate is negative → backward branch → predicted taken. If op(31)=0, forward branch → predicted not-taken (so bxx is false, not predicted). This is exactly "backward branches assumed taken, forward branches assumed not-taken."

2. `UncachedFetch` → `FetchControl` + `Fetcher` + `FetchReorderBuffer` + `InstructionBuffer`.
   - `FetchControl.PredictJump` (line 353): detects jal (1101111) and bxx (1100011 with op(31) set and op(14,13)!=1). Same backward-taken heuristic.

So both implement the same prediction heuristic. The `FetchControl` is the newer path. 

Now, which is the "active" one? Let me check the Core. Let me search.

Actually, let me reconsider. The lecture spec lists the minimal modules as:
- Fetch.scala
- FetchControlSpec.scala (this is the TEST for FetchControl)
- InstructionBuffer.scala

And key source files additionally include:
- FetchReorderBufferSpec.scala (test)
- UncachedFetch.scala

So the intent is: explain Fetch.scala (L0 cached fetch with prediction), FetchControl (via its test spec), FetchReorderBuffer (via its test spec), InstructionBuffer.

The practice task says: "阅读 Fetch.scala 与 InstructionBuffer，画出每周期最多取多少条指令、缓冲深度多少；再用 FetchControlSpec 解释一条向后分支在预测正确/错误时各自的控制流变化。"

OK so the focus is clearly:
1. Fetch.scala — how many instructions per cycle (wide channel), L0 cache
2. InstructionBuffer — buffer depth
3. FetchControlSpec — backward branch prediction behavior

Let me determine the actual numbers:
- Per-cycle fetch: `fetchDataBits / instructionBits = 256/32 = 8` instructions fetched per memory access (fetch line). This is `fetchInstrSlots` = 8 (or `lanes` in Fetch.scala).
- But `instructionLanes = 4` decoders. So how many instructions go to decode per cycle? In Fetch.scala, `io.inst.lanes` has `p.instructionLanes = 4` entries. So 4 instructions per cycle feed the 4 decoders.

Wait, in Fetch.scala line 84-86: `instValid`, `instAddr`, `instBits` are `Vec(p.instructionLanes, ...)` = 4 lanes. And the output `io.inst.lanes(i)` for i in 0 until instructionLanes (line 402). So Fetch outputs 4 instructions per cycle to the decoders.

In UncachedFetch, line 524: `io.inst.lanes <> instructionBuffer.io.out.take(4)`. So it takes only the first 4 of the buffer's outputs. And `window = p.fetchInstrSlots * 2 = 8*2 = 16`, and the buffer has `n = p.fetchInstrSlots = 8` output ports. So buffer depth is 16, 8 visible.

Hmm wait, `InstructionBuffer(gen, n, window)` — n=8 (number of slots, output ports), window=16 (capacity). So buffer capacity = 16 instructions, 8 visible at output.

Let me re-read the FetchControl spec to confirm the fetch behavior:
- "AlignedSpeculativeFetch" test: starts at PC 0x20000000, fetches 0x20000000, then 0x20000020, then 0x20000040. The step is 0x20 = 32 bytes = 8 instructions × 4 bytes. So each fetch is a 32-byte line = 8 instructions. This confirms `fetchDataBits = 256` (32 bytes) = 8 instructions per fetch.

So:
- Each fetch request brings in 256 bits = 32 bytes = 8 instructions (`fetchInstrSlots`).
- The 4 decoders consume 4 instructions per cycle (`instructionLanes`).
- InstructionBuffer depth (capacity/window) = 16, with 8 visible output ports.

Now let me verify how many instructions per cycle reach decode. In Fetch.scala, 4 lanes. In UncachedFetch, `take(4)`. So 4 instructions/cycle to decode. But the "4发射" (4-issue) refers to instructionLanes=4.

Let me clarify the "wide channel": the fetch bus (`ibus`) is 256-bit (fetchDataBits). Each fetch returns 8 instructions. The L0 cache line is also 256 bits (32 bytes), with 32 lines (indices) = 1024 bytes = 1KB.

Now I should look at the Core to confirm which fetch unit is used. Let me check.

Actually, let me look at how the Core selects between Fetch and UncachedFetch based on enableFetchL0. Let me grep.

Let me also double check: the lecture is "intermediate" level, building on u4-l1 (Parameters). I have the u4-l1 summary: "fetchInstrSlots=8、dbusSize=6", bare core 256-bit, SoC 128-bit.

Let me now check the Core for fetch instantiation and confirm the branch prediction in FetchControl test. Let me also read the microarch.md fetch section if any. Let me do these in parallel.

Now I have the key fact:
- `SCore.scala:57`: `val fetch = if (p.enableFetchL0) { Fetch(p) } else { Module(new UncachedFetch(p)) }`

So:
- When `enableFetchL0 = true` (default for bare core), the cached `Fetch` (with L0 cache) is used.
- When `enableFetchL0 = false` (production SoC, per flags.bzl and prod/BUILD), the `UncachedFetch` (FetchControl + Fetcher + FetchReorderBuffer + InstructionBuffer) is used.

This is a crucial insight! The production SoC uses `enableFetchL0 = False` → UncachedFetch path. From u3-l1 summary: "enableFetchL0（生产 SoC ... 取指不带 L0）". And from u4-l1 summary: "enableRvv/enableFloat/enableFetchL0".

So the two paths:
1. `Fetch` (L0 cached): default bare core, has integrated L0 cache, inline branch predecode (PredecodeDe), directly feeds 4 decoders (no InstructionBuffer).
2. `UncachedFetch` (no L0): production SoC, uses FetchControl (PC gen + prediction) → Fetcher (with FetchReorderBuffer for 2 outstanding) → InstructionBuffer (window=16) → 4 decoders.

file.content:
# CoralNPU Microarchitecture

![image](../images/microarch.png)

## Pipeline

The CoralNPU base processor is an in-order three-stage pipeline capable of
dispatching up to 4 instructions per cycle. The instruction stages are:

* **Instruction fetch:** Instructions are fetched from memory into an
instruction buffer.
* **Decode/Dispatch:** The first 4 instructions in the instruction buffer are
decoded. Interlock and scoreboard logic determine which of the instructions can
be dispatched this cycle. Instructions are forwarded to their respective
execution units.
* **Execute/Writeback:** Execution units with dispatched instructions read
operands from the register file and perform computation. Results can also be
written back to the register file in the same cycle.

Some execution units can take multiple cycles to execute. Instruction latencies
can be found in the table below:

| Instruction Type | Latency (cycles) | Description            |
| ---------------- | ---------------- | ---------------------- |
| Alu              | 1                | Add, sub, xor, ...     |
| Csr              | 1                | CSR instructions       |
| Bru              | 1                | bge, jal, ebreak, ...  |
| Mlu              | 2                | mul, mulh, ...         |
| Dvu              | Variable         | div, rem, ...          |
| Lsu              | 2+               | lw, sw, ...            |

# 取指、指令缓冲与重排序

## 1. 本讲目标
- 理解 CoralNPU 标量核前端如何「每周期搬入一整条宽指令行」并把指令稳定地喂给 4 个译码器
- 掌握两条取指路径（带 L0 缓存的 Fetch / 不带缓存的 UncachedFetch）的取舍
- 掌握「向后跳 taken、向前跳 not-taken」的静态分支预测策略及其预测错误时的冲刷惩罚
- 理解 InstructionBuffer 如何在取指与派发之间做弹性解耦
- 了解 FetchReorderBuffer 如何在总线响应乱序时把指令按序交还

## 2. 前置知识
- RISC-V 基本指令格式（I/B/J 型），特别是 B 型条件分支和 J 型无条件跳转的立即数编码
- 「PC」「指令」「译码」「派发」「冒险」等术语（来自前面讲义）
- Chisel 基础：Reg/Vec/Decoupled/valid-ready 握手
- 来自 u4-l1：Parameters 是配置单一真相源；fetchDataBits 默认 256、instructionLanes=4、enableFetchL0 开关
- 顺序流水线概念

## 3. 本讲源码地图
表格列出文件。

## 4. 核心概念与源码讲解

### 4.1 取指全景与两条取指路径
- 概念：取指是流水线第一阶段；CoralNPU 标量核是 in-order 3 级流水，每周期最多派发 4 条。
- 两条路径：SCore.scala:57 选择 Fetch（带 L0）或 UncachedFetch（不带 L0）。生产 SoC 关 L0（enableFetchL0=False）走 UncachedFetch。
- FetchUnit 抽象基类 IO：ibus（取指总线）、inst.lanes（4 条给译码）、branch（执行单元回报的实际跳转）、csr（复位 PC）。
- 流程：PC 生成 → ibus 取一行 → 预译码识别分支 → 喂 inst.lanes / 经 InstructionBuffer 缓冲 → 译码。
- 源码精读：SCore.scala:57, FetchUnit Interfaces 74-80, microarch 7-18.
- 代码实践：在仓库里找两条路径的开关，确认 core_mini_axi_sim 用哪条。
- 小练习

### 4.2 宽通道取指：一次搬入 8 条指令
- 概念：fetchDataBits=256 位 = 32 字节 = 8 条 32 位指令 = fetchInstrSlots。ibus.rdata 是 256 位宽。一行 = 一个 fetch line。
- 但译码器只有 4 个（instructionLanes=4），每周期消费 4 条。所以 Fetch 直出 4 条/cycle；UncachedFetch 经 InstructionBuffer 出 4 条/cycle。
- L0 缓存（Fetch.scala）：1KB（fetchCacheBytes=1024），32 行 × 32 字节，4... 不，是直接映射？tag|index|word。tag/index/word 划分。命中即可单周期出指令，miss 才发 ibus。
- 关键数：lanes=8（input lanes），indices=32。L0 cache 组织 lines 53-72。
- 源码精读：Parameters 130/131-136/126, Fetch.scala 56/53-72/78-81/402-407.
- 代码实践：算 L0 cache 的 index/tag 位宽，对照 Fetch.scala 64-72 的 assert。
- 小练习

### 4.3 分支预测：向后 taken、向前 not-taken
- 概念：静态预测。循环（向后跳，B 型立即数符号位=1）通常 taken；if/else（向前跳，符号位=0）通常 not-taken。
- 预测对象：JAL（无条件，必跳）、JALR→ra（return，链接端口预测）、B 型条件分支且向后（op(31)=1）。
- 源码：Fetch.scala PredecodeDe 325-336，bxx 329-330；FetchControl.PredictJump 353-366。
- 预测机制：在预译码阶段识别后，下一 PC 直接指向目标（taken）或顺序（not-taken），并设置 flushTx 冲刷错误路径。
- 预测正确 vs 错误：
  - JAL/向后 taken 预测正确：直接重定向到目标，继续投机取指，无损失。
  - 向后分支实际 not-taken（预测错误）：需要执行单元（BRU）回报 branch，冲刷已投机取入的指令，从顺序地址重新取。惩罚 = 投机取的行数。
  - 向前分支实际 taken（预测错误）：同理，BRU 回报，冲刷，从目标重取。
- FetchControlSpec 里的证据：
  - "FetchJump"（114-132）：inst(3)=JAL offset+32，预译码 hasJumped→flushTx→重定向到 0x2000002c，投机继续。
  - "Branch"（46-74）：外部 branch 重定向到 0x30000000，解决后投机取 0x30000020。
  - "AlignedSpeculativeFetch"（76-93）：顺序投机取 0x20000000→0x20000020→0x20000040。
- 关于实际条件分支预测错误的处理：由执行单元 BRU 经 io.branch 回报（FetchUnit.branch 接口），FetchControl 检测到 io.branch.valid 会冲刷并重定向（currentBranchOrFlush）。这部分 FetchControlSpec 没有直接覆盖条件分支预测错误的端到端测试，但其冲刷/重定向机制由 "Branch" 测试覆盖（外部 branch 模拟执行单元的纠正）。
- 代码实践：手动构造一条向后 B 型分支指令的 32 位编码，验证 op(31)=1；阅读 FetchControlSpec "FetchJump" 推演 JAL 重定向时序。
- 小练习：给一条 beq x0,x0,-8（向后）和 beq x0,x0,+12（向前），判断预测；算预测错误时的惩罚周期。

### 4.4 指令缓冲 InstructionBuffer
- 概念：弹性 FIFO，解耦「取指速率」与「派发速率」。取指一次来 8 条，派发每周期走 1~4 条，且后端可能反压。
- 数据结构：CircularBufferMulti（环形队列），capacity=window，n 个入/出端口。
- UncachedFetch 里：window = fetchInstrSlots*2 = 16，n = fetchInstrSlots = 8（8 个输出端口），但只 take(4) 喂译码。
- 入队：feedIn.nReady = min(nSpace, n)；保证不溢出。
- 出队：前 nEnqueued 条有效，OneHotInOrder 断言保证「按序派发」。
- 无反压？注释提到入队后下一拍才可见，使用时要注意。但 InstructionBuffer 本身通过 nReady 给取指端反压（CommitBackpressure 测试）。
- 源码精读：InstructionBuffer 36-48/54-57/63-71，CircularBufferMulti 48-77，UncachedFetch 520-525。
- FetchControlSpec "CommitBackpressure"（134-162）：bufferRequest.nReady=4（只能接 4 条），fetchData.ready=false（提交被阻），nReady 恢复 8 后才放行。证明取指提交受缓冲空间反压。
- 代码实践：画 InstructionBuffer 容量/可见端口/实际喂译码数；用 CommitBackpressure 测试解释反压。
- 小练习

### 4.5 取指重排序缓冲 FetchReorderBuffer
- 概念：Fetcher 允许 2 条取指事务同时在途（maxConcurrentTx=2）。ibus 的 rdata 是寄存的（下一拍才到），且多条在途时响应可能乱序。FetchReorderBuffer 按 txid 匹配响应、按入队顺序（FIFO）提交，把乱序响应重排成顺序交付。
- 另一作用：注释 293-295 说 reorderBuffer 不 flow response，作为打拍延迟，打断 rdata→addr 的组合环。
- 数据结构：queue(capacity) 个 Entry，每个含 txid/addr/resp(Valid)。trySaveResponse 按 txid 匹配写入 resp。commit 从 queue(0) 头部出队，要求 head 已响应且未被 cancel。
- 关键时序（5 级：resp→commit→flush→discard→enq），flowResponse=false 时 commit 寄存一拍。
- FetchReorderBufferSpec "Reorder responses"（211-251）：Tx1/2/3 入队，响应以 3/2/1 乱序到达，但提交严格按 1/2/3 顺序。这就是「重排序」的核心证据。
- 注意：测试用 capacity=4 做充分覆盖，实际 Fetcher 用 capacity=maxConcurrentTx=2。
- flush 语义：分支预测错误/冲刷时，未响应的 entry 静默丢弃，已响应的 entry 经 freeTxid 回收 txid。
- 源码精读：UncachedFetch Fetcher 290-302/293-295 注释；FetchReorderBuffer 39-98 io/Entry/123-143 trySaveResponse/236 s2Valid；FetchReorderBufferSpec 211-251。
- 代码实践：读 "Reorder responses" 测试，画出 3 个 txid 的入队顺序、响应到达顺序、提交顺序对照表；解释为何提交顺序与入队顺序一致。
- 小练习

## 5. 综合实践
设计：给一段含循环的小汇编片段，要求读者：(a) 标出每条指令地址；(b) 模拟前若干周期的取指 PC 序列（含向后分支预测 taken 后的投机重定向）；(c) 假设某次循环退出（向后分支实际 not-taken，预测错误），描述 FetchControl 如何经 io.branch 冲刷 InstructionBuffer 并重取；(d) 标出 FetchReorderBuffer 在此过程中的容量与提交顺序。或者更可操作：在 FetchControlSpec 里新写一个测试用例的伪代码（不实际运行），驱动一条「预测错误→冲刷→重取」的序列并写出每拍 fetchAddr 的期望值。我会做成「源码阅读 + 画图 + 手动推演」型。

实际上，要可操作且不假装运行，我设计成：基于 FetchControlSpec 已有的 "Branch" 测试，让读者把它改写成「先投机取顺序地址，再注入一个 branch（模拟 BRU 纠正），观察冲刷与重定向」，并写出每拍 fetchAddr 的预期。标注「待本地验证」。

## 6. 本讲小结
3-6 bullets.

## 7. 下一步学习建议
下一讲 u4-l3 译码；或继续读 FetchControl 的 PC 状态机；或读 SCore 看取指与派发的衔接。

input.content:
# 取指、指令缓冲与重排序

## 1. 本讲目标

本讲聚焦 CoralNPU 标量核流水线的第一阶段——**取指（Fetch）**。读完本讲你应该能够：

1. 说清「一次取指搬入多少条指令、每周期送给译码器多少条」，理解 256 位宽通道与 4 发射之间的关系。
2. 区分项目里**两条取指实现**——带 L0 缓存的 `Fetch` 与不带缓存的 `UncachedFetch`——以及生产 SoC 选择哪一条。
3. 掌握 CoralNPU 的静态分支预测策略：**向后跳预测 taken、向前跳预测 not-taken**，并能讲清预测正确与错误两种情况下的控制流差别与冲刷惩罚。
4. 理解 `InstructionBuffer` 如何在「取指速率」与「派发速率」之间做弹性解耦与反压。
5. 理解 `FetchReorderBuffer` 为什么能在总线响应乱序时把指令**按原顺序**交还给后续阶段。

## 2. 前置知识

- **RISC-V 指令编码基础**：每条标准指令 32 位；条件分支（B 型，opcode `1100011`）与无条件跳转（JAL，opcode `1101111`）的立即数里都含一个**符号位**，符号位为 1 表示跳转目标在「当前 PC 之前」（向后），为 0 表示在「之后」（向前）。
- **顺序流水线术语**：PC、取指、译码、派发、冒险、反压、冲刷（flush）。这些在第 4 单元开篇讲义里已建立。
- **Chisel 基础**：`Reg`、`Vec`、`Decoupled`（valid/ready/bits 握手）、`Valid`（只有 valid+bits，无 ready）。
- **来自 u4-l1 的认知**：`Parameters` 是内核配置的单一真相源；裸核默认 `fetchDataBits = 256`、`instructionLanes = 4`，并有 `enableFetchL0`、`fetchInstrSlots` 等开关。本讲所有「数字」都以这些参数为依据。
- **来自 u3 系列的认知**：取指最终要经 `IBus2Axi` 走到 ITCM 或外部 AXI；本讲只关心「核内取指单元」这一段。

## 4. 核心概念与源码讲解

### 4.1 取指全景与两条取指路径

#### 4.1.1 概念说明

取指是流水线的最上游，它的任务是：**持续地把「接下来要执行的指令」喂给译码器，且尽量每周期喂满 4 条**。CoralNPU 标量核是一条 in-order 三级流水，每周期最多派发 4 条指令（见 [microarch.md:7-18](https://github.com/google-coral/coralnpu/blob/a8281b0d5701d8939cf9ea424b1cd34e18c43230/doc/microarch/microarch.md#L7-L18)）。要让这 4 个译码器不挨饿，取指必须有「宽通道」——一次从存储里搬回不止一条指令。

项目里存在**两条并列的取指实现**，由一个参数二选一：

- **`Fetch`（带 L0 指令缓存）**：默认实现，内部有一块 1KB 的小 L0 cache，命中时单周期出指令，miss 才向总线发请求；预译码与分支预测都内联其中。
- **`UncachedFetch`（不带 L0 缓存）**：把取指拆成 `FetchControl`（产生 PC、做分支预测）+ `Fetcher`（发总线请求、含重排序缓冲）+ `InstructionBuffer`（缓冲）三段，结构更清晰、可观测性更好。

#### 4.1.2 核心流程

两条路径对外都满足同一个抽象接口 `FetchUnit`，下游（译码器）看到的都是 `io.inst.lanes`（4 条 `Decoupled` 指令）。粗略数据流：

```
CSR(复位PC) ─▶ PC生成(+分支预测) ─▶ ibus取一行(256bit=8条指令)
                                        │
                          ┌─────────────┴─────────────┐
                  Fetch: L0缓存 + 预译码         UncachedFetch:
                  直接出 4 条到 lanes            FetchControl→Fetcher(ROB)→InstructionBuffer→取4条到 lanes
                                        │
                                   io.inst.lanes (4条) ─▶ 译码/派发
```

选择逻辑只有一行，但它是理解整章的钥匙：[SCore.scala:57](https://github.com/google-coral/coralnpu/blob/a8281b0d5701d8939cf9ea424b1cd34e18c43230/hdl/chisel/src/coralnpu/scalar/SCore.scala#L57)。

#### 4.1.3 源码精读

标量核顶层依据 `enableFetchL0` 在两条路径间选择：

[SCore.scala:57](https://github.com/google-coral/coralnpu/blob/a8281b0d5701d8939cf9ea424b1cd34e18c43230/hdl/chisel/src/coralnpu/scalar/SCore.scala#L57-L57) —— `Fetch(p)`（带 L0）与 `UncachedFetch(p)`（不带 L0）二选一。

参数默认值：[Parameters.scala:125](https://github.com/google-coral/coralnpu/blob/a8281b0d5701d8939cf9ea424b1cd34e18c43230/hdl/chisel/src/coralnpu/Parameters.scala#L125-L125) 默认 `enableFetchL0 = true`；但**生产 SoC 的构建脚本里显式关掉它**（如 [hdl/chisel/src/coralnpu/flags.bzl:18](https://github.com/google-coral/coralnpu/blob/a8281b0d5701d8939cf9ea424b1cd34e18c43230/hdl/chisel/src/coralnpu/flags.bzl#L18-L18) 与 `prod/BUILD` 都传 `--enableFetchL0=False`），也就是说**生产 SoC 走的是 `UncachedFetch` 这条更可观测的路径**。这与 u3-l1 提到的「生产 SoC 取指不带 L0」一致。

两条路径共享的抽象接口：[Interfaces.scala:74-80](https://github.com/google-coral/coralnpu/blob/a8281b0d5701d8939cf9ea424b1cd34e18c43230/hdl/chisel/src/coralnpu/Interfaces.scala#L74-L80) 定义了 `FetchUnit`，其关键端口是 `ibus`（取指总线）、`inst.lanes`（4 条给译码器的指令）、`branch`（执行单元回报的**实际**跳转，用于纠正预测）、`csr`（提供复位 PC）。

取指总线接口：[Interfaces.scala:51-62](https://github.com/google-coral/coralnpu/blob/a8281b0d5701d8939cf9ea424b1cd34e18c43230/hdl/chisel/src/coralnpu/Interfaces.scala#L51-L62) 定义 `IBusIO`，一次请求带 `addr`，返回 `rdata`（宽 `fetchDataBits` 位）和 `fault`。注意它是「地址一拍、数据下一拍」的总线模型。

#### 4.1.4 代码实践

**目标**：确认两种构建配置分别走哪条取指路径。

1. 打开 `hdl/chisel/src/coralnpu/scalar/SCore.scala:57`，确认选择语句。
2. 用 `grep -rn "enableFetchL0" hdl/chisel/src/coralnpu` 列出所有设置点，找出哪些构建目标把它设为 `False`。
3. 对照 `flags.bzl` 与 `prod/BUILD`，得出「生产 SoC 用 `UncachedFetch`、裸核仿真用 `Fetch`」的结论。
4. **预期结果**：你会看到 `core_mini_axi_sim`（主力仿真器）相关的生产配置里 `enableFetchL0=False`，因此后续 4.4、4.5 两节里讲到的 `InstructionBuffer` 与 `FetchReorderBuffer` 在生产路径里是真实启用的。

#### 4.1.5 小练习与答案

**练习 1**：为什么本项目要同时维护两条取指路径，而不是只留一条？
**答案**：`Fetch` 的 L0 缓存追求「命中即出指令」的低延迟，但状态多、可观测性差；`UncachedFetch` 把 PC 生成、总线请求、缓冲、重排拆成独立模块，便于验证与排错（你能单独仿真 `FetchControl`、`FetchReorderBuffer`）。生产 SoC 偏好后者，裸核默认保留前者。

**练习 2**：下游译码器需要关心当前用的是哪条取指路径吗？
**答案**：不需要。两者都实现 `FetchUnit`，对下游暴露的 `io.inst.lanes`（4 条 `Decoupled[FetchInstruction]`）完全一致——这正是抽象接口的意义。

---

### 4.2 宽通道取指：一次搬入 8 条指令

#### 4.2.1 概念说明

「4 发射」不等于「每周期只取 4 条」。CoralNPU 的取指总线是 **256 位宽**，一条 32 位指令，所以**一次取指事务（一行 fetch line）能搬回 8 条指令**。但译码器只有 4 个，每周期只能消费 4 条。于是取指端的「带宽」是译码端的 2 倍，留出余量去吸收分支重定向与缓存 miss 带来的气泡。

这里出现三个容易混淆的「宽度」参数，务必分清：

| 参数（Parameters.scala） | 值 | 含义 |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `fetchDataBits`（[:130](https://github.com/google-coral/coralnpu/blob/a8281b0d5701d8939cf9ea424b1cd34e18c43230/hdl/chisel/src/coralnpu/Parameters.scala#L130-L130)） | 256 | 取指总线一次返回的位宽 = 32 字节 |
| `fetchInstrSlots`（[:131-136](https://github.com/google-coral/coralnpu/blob/a8281b0d5701d8939cf9ea424b1cd34e18c43230/hdl/chisel/src/coralnpu/Parameters.scala#L131-L136)） | \(256/32 = 8\) | 一行里的指令条数 |
| `instructionLanes`（[:73](https://github.com/google-coral/coralnpu/blob/a8281b0d5701d8939cf9ea424b1cd34e18c43230/hdl/chisel/src/coralnpu/Parameters.scala#L73-L73)） | 4 | 每周期送进译码器的指令条数 = 译码器个数 |

> 关键关系：\(\text{fetchInstrSlots} = \text{fetchDataBits} / \text{instructionBits}\)；\(\text{instructionLanes} = 4\) 决定每周期译码 4 条。SoC 配置里 `fetchDataBits` 可能降到 128（即 4 条/行），但本节以裸核默认 256 为例。

#### 4.2.2 核心流程

`Fetch`（带 L0）的取指节奏：

```
每周期：
  1. 用「当前 PC」查 L0 cache（按 tag/index 命中判断）
  2. 命中 → 从 L0 单周期读出 4 条到 inst.lanes；miss 且该行未在途 → 发 ibus 请求
  3. ibus 返回 256 位 → 写入 L0 对应行 → 后续周期从 L0 出指令
  4. 译码器每周期消费最多 4 条，PC 沿用未消费部分 + 跨行续取
```

`UncachedFetch`（不带 L0）则不缓存，每行只用一次：`FetchControl` 顺序递增 PC（每次 +32 字节），`Fetcher` 发 ibus，回来的 8 条经预译码后**入 `InstructionBuffer`**，再从缓冲里每周期取 4 条给译码。

#### 4.2.3 源码精读

`Fetch.scala` 文件头注释一针见血：[Fetch.scala:16-18](https://github.com/google-coral/coralnpu/blob/a8281b0d5701d8939cf9ea424b1cd34e18c43230/hdl/chisel/src/coralnpu/scalar/Fetch.scala#L16-L18) 说明它是「4 路取指器，直接喂 4 个译码器；内含部分译码器识别分支，向后分支假定 taken、向前分支假定 not-taken」。

L0 缓存的几何参数在此计算：[Fetch.scala:56-62](https://github.com/google-coral/coralnpu/blob/a8281b0d5701d8939cf9ea424b1cd34e18c43230/hdl/chisel/src/coralnpu/scalar/Fetch.scala#L56-L62) —— `lanes = fetchDataBits/instructionBits = 8`（一行 8 条），`indices = fetchCacheBytes*8/fetchDataBits = 32`（L0 共 32 行）。L0 地址被切成 `Tag | Index | word偏移`，与经典直接映射 cache 一致。

`Fetch.scala:64-72` 用一组 `assert` 把「1KB / 256 位 / 32 位指令」这套数字钉死，可作为对照基准：`indexLsb==5, indexMsb==9, tagLsb==10, indices==32, lanes==8`，即 L0 是 32 行 × 32 字节 = 1024 字节。

L0 的存储体：[Fetch.scala:78-81](https://github.com/google-coral/coralnpu/blob/a8281b0d5701d8939cf9ea424b1cd34e18c43230/hdl/chisel/src/coralnpu/scalar/Fetch.scala#L78-L81) 定义了有效位 `l0valid`、在途请求位 `l0req`、标签阵列 `l0tag`、数据阵列 `l0data`（每行 256 位）。

最终输出给译码器：[Fetch.scala:402-407](https://github.com/google-coral/coralnpu/blob/a8281b0d5701d8939cf9ea424b1cd34e18c43230/hdl/chisel/src/coralnpu/scalar/Fetch.scala#L402-L407) 把 `instValid/instAddr/instBits` 接到 `io.inst.lanes(i)`，循环上界是 `p.instructionLanes = 4`——确认 **L0 路径每周期输出 4 条**。紧接其后的 [Fetch.scala:410-412](https://github.com/google-coral/coralnpu/blob/a8281b0d5701d8939cf9ea424b1cd34e18c43230/hdl/chisel/src/coralnpu/scalar/Fetch.scala#L410-L412) 用断言保证这 4 条指令的地址是连续的（`instAddr(0) + i*4 === instAddr(i)`），即 4 路同周期取的是相邻指令。

#### 4.2.4 代码实践

**目标**：亲手算出 L0 cache 的位宽划分，并与源码断言对齐。

1. 读 [Parameters.scala:126](https://github.com/google-coral/coralnpu/blob/a8281b0d5701d8939cf9ea424b1cd34e18c43230/hdl/chisel/src/coralnpu/Parameters.scala#L126-L126)（`fetchCacheBytes=1024`）与 [:130](https://github.com/google-coral/coralnpu/blob/a8281b0d5701d8939cf9ea424b1cd34e18c43230/hdl/chisel/src/coralnpu/Parameters.scala#L130-L130)（`fetchDataBits=256`）。
2. 自行计算：行数 \(= 1024 \times 8 / 256 = 32\)；每行字节数 \(= 256/8 = 32\)；index 位数 \(= \log_2 32 = 5\)。
3. 对照 [Fetch.scala:64-72](https://github.com/google-coral/coralnpu/blob/a8281b0d5701d8939cf9ea424b1cd34e18c43230/hdl/chisel/src/coralnpu/scalar/Fetch.scala#L64-L72) 的 6 条 `assert`，逐条验证你的计算。
4. **预期结果**：`indexLsb=5, indexMsb=9, tagLsb=10, indices=32, indexCountBits=5, lanes=8` 全部与你的手算一致。

#### 4.2.5 小练习与答案

**练习 1**：如果把 `fetchDataBits` 从 256 改成 128，`fetchInstrSlots` 与 L0 行数各变成多少？
**答案**：`fetchInstrSlots = 128/32 = 4`（一行 4 条）；L0 行数 \(= 1024\times8/128 = 64\)。这正是 SoC 配置采用更窄通道时的情形。

**练习 2**：一行能搬 8 条，译码器每周期只吃 4 条，多出来的 4 条在 L0 路径里存在哪里？
**答案**：存在 L0 数据阵列 `l0data` 里（整行 256 位都被缓存）。下一周期 PC 仍指向同行的后半段，再次查 L0 命中，继续读出后续 4 条，无需再发总线请求。

---

### 4.3 分支预测：向后 taken、向前 not-taken

#### 4.3.1 概念说明

CoralNPU 标量核是**不投机**（non-speculative）的执行核——它不会在分支未决之前提前执行分支后的指令。但**取指端**仍然做了简单的**静态预测**：在「预译码」阶段只看指令编码本身，就决定下一条 PC 取在哪里，从而让取指流不被分支打断。这套策略的直觉非常朴素：

- **向后跳（循环回退）→ 预测 taken**：循环体末尾跳回开头的分支，绝大多数循环迭代里都是要跳的。
- **向前跳（if 跳过 then 分支）→ 预测 not-taken**：向前跳常用于「跳过一段代码」，多数情况不跳，于是顺序取指。

注意：这是**取指方向的预测**，不改变「执行不投机」的语义。一旦执行单元（BRU）算出真实分支结果，若与预测不符，就经 `io.branch` 回报，冲刷取指路径并重定向——这部分是 4.4 节缓冲 flush 与本节「预测错误惩罚」的来源。

#### 4.3.2 核心流程

预测发生在「预译码」环节，两条路径各自有一份逻辑（结论一致）：

```
对刚取回的每条指令做轻量解码：
  - JAL   (opcode 1101111)        → 无条件跳转，必 taken，目标 = PC + J 立即数
  - JALR→ra (return, 需 linkPort) → 返回预测，目标来自 linkPort
  - B 型 (opcode 1100011) 且 op(31)=1 且 非保留编码 → 向后条件分支，预测 taken
  - 其余（含向前 B 型）            → 预测 not-taken，顺序取指

取指 PC 更新：
  - 命中一个预测 taken 的跳转 → 下一 PC 指向目标，并置 flushTx 冲刷「错误路径」上已取的指令
  - 否则顺序向前取下一行（投机续取）
```

> B 型立即数的符号位正是 `inst(31)`（即 `imm[12]`）。它为 1 表示负偏移（目标在当前 PC 之前 = 向后）。所以 `op(31)` 这一位就承担了「向后判定」。

预测正确与错误的差别：

- **预测正确**（如循环回退确实 taken）：取指流直接从目标继续，投机续取，**几乎无损失**。
- **预测错误**（如循环退出那一拍，向后分支实际 not-taken；或向前分支实际 taken）：错误路径上的指令已经被取进缓冲，需要由 BRU 经 `io.branch` 回报真实目标，`FetchControl` 检测到 `branch.valid` 后**冲刷（flush）已投机指令**并从正确地址重取。惩罚 ≈ 「错误路径上已取的行数 × 处理延迟」。

#### 4.3.3 源码精读

`Fetch`（L0 路径）的预译码函数：[Fetch.scala:325-336](https://github.com/google-coral/coralnpu/blob/a8281b0d5701d8939cf9ea424b1cd34e18c43230/hdl/chisel/src/coralnpu/scalar/Fetch.scala#L325-L336) `PredecodeDe`。其中关键是「向后条件分支」的判定：

```scala
val bxx = op === BitPat("b???????_?????_?????_???_?????_1100011") &&
            op(31) && op(14,13) =/= 1.U   // op(31)=1 → 向后 → 预测 taken
```

[Fetch.scala:329-330](https://github.com/google-coral/coralnpu/blob/a8281b0d5701d8939cf9ea424b1cd34e18c43230/hdl/chisel/src/coralnpu/scalar/Fetch.scala#L329-L330) 这两行就是「向后 taken」策略的字面来源；`op(14,13)=/=1` 仅是排除 B 型的保留编码。JAL（[:326](https://github.com/google-coral/coralnpu/blob/a8281b0d5701d8939cf9ea424b1cd34e18c43230/hdl/chisel/src/coralnpu/scalar/Fetch.scala#L326-L326)）与 return（[:327-328](https://github.com/google-coral/coralnpu/blob/a8281b0d5701d8939cf9ea424b1cd34e18c43230/hdl/chisel/src/coralnpu/scalar/Fetch.scala#L327-L328)）也在此一并预测。

`UncachedFetch` 路径里，`FetchControl` 用一份等价逻辑 `PredictJump`：[UncachedFetch.scala:353-366](https://github.com/google-coral/coralnpu/blob/a8281b0d5701d8939cf9ea424b1cd34e18c43230/hdl/chisel/src/coralnpu/scalar/UncachedFetch.scala#L353-L366)，其中 [:357-358](https://github.com/google-coral/coralnpu/blob/a8281b0d5701d8939cf9ea424b1cd34e18c43230/hdl/chisel/src/coralnpu/scalar/UncachedFetch.scala#L357-L358) 同样用 `inst(31)` 判定向后。

`FetchControl` 的 PC 生成与投机续取：[UncachedFetch.scala:463-472](https://github.com/google-coral/coralnpu/blob/a8281b0d5701d8939cf9ea424b1cd34e18c43230/hdl/chisel/src/coralnpu/scalar/UncachedFetch.scala#L463-L472) 的 `pcNext` 决定树——分支/冲刷优先，其次「取到的指令含跳转」则用预测目标（[:467](https://github.com/google-coral/coralnpu/blob/a8281b0d5701d8939cf9ea424b1cd34e18c43230/hdl/chisel/src/coralnpu/scalar/UncachedFetch.scala#L467-L467)），否则顺序递增到下一行（[:469](https://github.com/google-coral/coralnpu/blob/a8281b0d5701d8939cf9ea424b1cd34e18c43230/hdl/chisel/src/coralnpu/scalar/UncachedFetch.scala#L469-L469)）。冲刷信号 `flushTx` 在 [:490](https://github.com/google-coral/coralnpu/blob/a8281b0d5701d8939cf9ea424b1cd34e18c43230/hdl/chisel/src/coralnpu/scalar/UncachedFetch.scala#L490-L490) 汇总：任何进行中分支/冲刷或刚写入缓冲的跳转都会触发。

行为证据来自 `FetchControlSpec`：

- **顺序投机续取**——[FetchControlSpec.scala:76-93](https://github.com/google-coral/coralnpu/blob/a8281b0d5701d8939cf9ea424b1cd34e18c43230/hdl/chisel/src/coralnpu/scalar/FetchControlSpec.scala#L76-L93) `AlignedSpeculativeFetch`：复位 PC=`0x20000000` 后，`fetchAddr` 依次给出 `0x20000000` → `0x20000020` → `0x20000040`，每拍 +0x20（32 字节 = 一行 8 条指令），证明**没有分支时取指投机地顺序前进**。
- **JAL 预测重定向**——[FetchControlSpec.scala:114-132](https://github.com/google-coral/coralnpu/blob/a8281b0d5701d8939cf9ea424b1cd34e18c43230/hdl/chisel/src/coralnpu/scalar/FetchControlSpec.scala#L114-L132) `FetchJump`：取回的一行里第 4 条（`inst(3)`）被置成 JAL、偏移 +32（`0x0200006f`）。预译码识别 `hasJumped`，触发 `flushTx`（[:125](https://github.com/google-coral/coralnpu/blob/a8281b0d5701d8939cf9ea424b1cd34e18c43230/hdl/chisel/src/coralnpu/scalar/FetchControlSpec.scala#L125-L125) 断言为真），随后 `fetchAddr` 重定向到目标 `0x2000002c`（= 该指令地址 `0x2000000c` + 32，[:130](https://github.com/google-coral/coralnpu/blob/a8281b0d5701d8939cf9ea424b1cd34e18c43230/hdl/chisel/src/coralnpu/scalar/FetchControlSpec.scala#L130-L130)）。
- **外部纠正重定向**——[FetchControlSpec.scala:46-74](https://github.com/google-coral/coralnpu/blob/a8281b0d5701d8939cf9ea424b1cd34e18c43230/hdl/chisel/src/coralnpu/scalar/FetchControlSpec.scala#L46-L74) `Branch`：注入 `branch.valid` 指向 `0x30000000`（模拟 BRU 回报真实跳转），`FetchControl` 在 [:65-66](https://github.com/google-coral/coralnpu/blob/a8281b0d5701d8939cf9ea424b1cd34e18c43230/hdl/chisel/src/coralnpu/scalar/FetchControlSpec.scala#L65-L66) 把 `fetchAddr` 切到 `0x30000000`，并在下一拍投机续取 `0x30000020`（[:71-72](https://github.com/google-coral/coralnpu/blob/a8281b0d5701d8939cf9ea424b1cd34e18c43230/hdl/chisel/src/coralnpu/scalar/FetchControlSpec.scala#L71-L72)）。这就是「预测错误被纠正」时重定向 + 继续投机的过程。

#### 4.3.4 代码实践

**目标**：用一个向后条件分支，手工走一遍「预测 taken、执行纠正」的时序。

1. 构造一条向后 `beq`，例如目标在当前 PC 前 8 字节。其 B 型立即数符号位 `imm[12]`（即 `inst(31)`）为 1。
2. 在 [FetchControl.scala:357-358](https://github.com/google-coral/coralnpu/blob/a8281b0d5701d8939cf9ea424b1cd34e18c43230/hdl/chisel/src/coralnpu/scalar/UncachedFetch.scala#L357-L358) 确认：因为 `inst(31)=1`，`bxx` 为真 → 该分支被预测 taken → `nextPc` 指向「向后」的目标地址。
3. **预测正确时**：循环每轮都跳，取指流稳定地在「目标…目标+若干行…回到目标」之间运行，无额外气泡。
4. **预测错误时**（循环退出那一拍，分支实际 not-taken）：参考 `Branch` 测试，向 `FetchControl.io.branch` 注入真实顺序地址，观察 `fetchAddr` 被纠正、`flushTx` 拉高、`InstructionBuffer` 被冲刷。
5. **待本地验证**：把 `FetchControlSpec` 的 `Branch` 用例改写成「先投机取若干顺序行、再注入 branch 纠正」，逐拍记录 `fetchAddr.bits`，应看到「顺序地址 → 纠正目标 → 目标+0x20 投机续取」的序列。

#### 4.3.5 小练习与答案

**练习 1**：一条 `beq x0,x0,-8`（向后 8 字节）和一条 `beq x0,x0,+12`（向前 12 字节），分别被预测成什么？
**答案**：前者 `inst(31)=1`（负偏移）→ 预测 taken；后者 `inst(31)=0`（正偏移）→ 不满足 `bxx` → 预测 not-taken（顺序取指）。

**练习 2**：为什么「向前分支预测 not-taken」对 `if/else` 结构通常是赚的？
**答案**：典型的 `if (cond) { then }` 编译后，`cond` 为假时跳过 then 块——这是「向前跳」。多数路径里 `cond` 为真、顺序执行 then 块，预测 not-taken 命中；只有 `cond` 为假时才预测错误、付出一次冲刷惩罚。

**练习 3**：JAL 是无条件跳转，为什么也算「预测」？
**答案**：JAL 的目标在译码阶段就能由立即数算出，取指端预译码后可以直接把 PC 重定向到目标，避免「取到 JAL 后那一行的无用指令」。它没有「对错」之分（必然 taken），但仍需要 `flushTx` 把 JAL 之后已投机取入的指令冲掉。

### 4.4 指令缓冲 InstructionBuffer

#### 4.4.1 概念说明

取指端「一次来 8 条」，派发端「每周期走 0~4 条，还可能因为记分板/执行单元忙而反压」——两边的速率并不匹配。`InstructionBuffer` 就是夹在中间的**弹性 FIFO**：吸收取指的突发，并向派发端提供稳定的「最多 4 条就绪指令」。它只出现在 `UncachedFetch` 路径里（`Fetch` 路径用 L0 cache + 输出寄存器扮演类似角色）。

#### 4.4.2 核心流程

`InstructionBuffer` 建立在通用环形队列 `CircularBufferMulti` 之上：

```
入队端（来自 FetchControl 的 bufferRequest，一次最多 8 条）：
  feedIn.nReady = min(队列剩余空间 nSpace, n)
  → 队列快满时反压取指端，停止接收新行

出队端（去往译码/派发，最多 4 条/cycle）：
  前 nEnqueued 条均可见，按地址顺序置 valid
  → 译码器按 ready 选择消费前若干条（OneHotInOrder 断言保证按序）

flush（分支重定向/冲刷）：
  清空整个队列，丢弃已投机取入的错误路径指令
```

> 容量关系：在 `UncachedFetch` 中，`window = fetchInstrSlots * 2 = 16`（缓冲深度 16 条），可见输出端口 `n = fetchInstrSlots = 8`，但**只取前 4 条喂译码**（`instructionLanes = 4`）。

#### 4.4.3 源码精读

`InstructionBuffer` 的定义与 IO：[InstructionBuffer.scala:36-48](https://github.com/google-coral/coralnpu/blob/a8281b0d5701d8939cf9ea424b1cd34e18c43230/hdl/chisel/src/common/InstructionBuffer.scala#L36-L48)，参数为 `gen`（元素类型）、`n`（入/出端口数）、`window`（容量）；断言 `window % n == 0`（[:39](https://github.com/google-coral/coralnpu/blob/a8281b0d5701d8939cf9ea424b1cd34e18c43230/hdl/chisel/src/common/InstructionBuffer.scala#L39-L39)）。

入队反压逻辑：[InstructionBuffer.scala:54-57](https://github.com/google-coral/coralnpu/blob/a8281b0d5701d8939cf9ea424b1cd34e18c43230/hdl/chisel/src/common/InstructionBuffer.scala#L54-L57) —— `feedInReady = min(nSpace, n)`，剩余空间不足 n 时只接收能容纳的部分，从源头避免溢出。

出队与「按序派发」保证：[InstructionBuffer.scala:63-71](https://github.com/google-coral/coralnpu/blob/a8281b0d5701d8939cf9ea424b1cd34e18c43230/hdl/chisel/src/common/InstructionBuffer.scala#L63-L71) —— 前 `nEnqueued` 个端口置 valid；[:69](https://github.com/google-coral/coralnpu/blob/a8281b0d5701d8939cf9ea424b1cd34e18c43230/hdl/chisel/src/common/InstructionBuffer.scala#L69-L69) 的 `assert(OneHotInOrder(io.out.map(_.fire)))` 强制「消费必须从队首连续进行」，不允许跳过前面的指令去取后面的——这正是 in-order 派发所要求的。

底层环形队列：[CircularBufferMulti.scala:48-77](https://github.com/google-coral/coralnpu/blob/a8281b0d5701d8939cf9ea424b1cd34e18c43230/hdl/chisel/src/common/CircularBufferMulti.scala#L48-L77)，用 `enqPtr/deqPtr/nEnqueued` 三个寄存器维护多端口入出，flush 时三者清零（[:67-69](https://github.com/google-coral/coralnpu/blob/a8281b0d5701d8939cf9ea424b1cd34e18c43230/hdl/chisel/src/common/CircularBufferMulti.scala#L67-L69)）。

`UncachedFetch` 里的实例化：[UncachedFetch.scala:520-525](https://github.com/google-coral/coralnpu/blob/a8281b0d5701d8939cf9ea424b1cd34e18c43230/hdl/chisel/src/coralnpu/scalar/UncachedFetch.scala#L520-L525) —— `window = fetchInstrSlots*2 = 16`，`io.inst.lanes <> instructionBuffer.io.out.take(4)`（**8 个输出端口里只取前 4 个**喂译码），并在 [:525](https://github.com/google-coral/coralnpu/blob/a8281b0d5701d8939cf9ea424b1cd34e18c43230/hdl/chisel/src/coralnpu/scalar/UncachedFetch.scala#L525-L525) 把「iflush / branch / debug_pc」三类事件都接到 `flush`，实现预测错误时的整队冲刷。

反压行为的仿真证据：[FetchControlSpec.scala:134-162](https://github.com/google-coral/coralnpu/blob/a8281b0d5701d8939cf9ea424b1cd34e18c43230/hdl/chisel/src/coralnpu/scalar/FetchControlSpec.scala#L134-L162) `CommitBackpressure`：当 `bufferRequest.nReady` 只有 4（缓冲只剩 4 个空位）时，`fetchData.ready` 被拉低（[:152](https://github.com/google-coral/coralnpu/blob/a8281b0d5701d8939cf9ea424b1cd34e18c43230/hdl/chisel/src/coralnpu/scalar/FetchControlSpec.scala#L152-L152)）——**取指结果无法提交**；等 `nReady` 恢复到 8，`fetchData.ready` 才变高、`nValid` 一下变为 8（[:159-160](https://github.com/google-coral/coralnpu/blob/a8281b0d5701d8939cf9ea424b1cd34e18c43230/hdl/chisel/src/coralnpu/scalar/FetchControlSpec.scala#L159-L160)）。注意 [:143](https://github.com/google-coral/coralnpu/blob/a8281b0d5701d8939cf9ea424b1cd34e18c43230/hdl/chisel/src/coralnpu/scalar/FetchControlSpec.scala#L143-L143) 表明「发起新取指」并不被反压阻塞，被阻塞的只是「把取指结果写进缓冲」。

#### 4.4.4 代码实践

**目标**：画出 `InstructionBuffer` 的容量与端口关系，并用一个测试解释反压。

1. 读 [UncachedFetch.scala:520-525](https://github.com/google-coral/coralnpu/blob/a8281b0d5701d8939cf9ea424b1cd34e18c43230/hdl/chisel/src/coralnpu/scalar/UncachedFetch.scala#L520-L525)，填出下表：

   | 量 | 值 | 出处 |
   | --- | --- | --- |
   | 缓冲容量 window | 16 | `fetchInstrSlots*2` |
   | 入/出端口数 n | 8 | `fetchInstrSlots` |
   | 实际喂译码数 | 4 | `out.take(4)` |

2. 打开 `FetchControlSpec` 的 `CommitBackpressure`（[:134-162](https://github.com/google-coral/coralnpu/blob/a8281b0d5701d8939cf9ea424b1cd34e18c43230/hdl/chisel/src/coralnpu/scalar/FetchControlSpec.scala#L134-L162)），逐拍解释：为什么 `nReady=4` 时 `fetchData.ready=false`？
3. **预期结果**：因为 `sufficientBuffer = (nReady >= predecode.count)`（见 [FetchControl 的 :435-436](https://github.com/google-coral/coralnpu/blob/a8281b0d5701d8939cf9ea424b1cd34e18c43230/hdl/chisel/src/coralnpu/scalar/UncachedFetch.scala#L435-L436)），缓冲空间不足以容纳本次取回的指令条数时，取指结果就被挡在缓冲门外，直到派发端消费、腾出空间。

#### 4.4.5 小练习与答案

**练习 1**：`InstructionBuffer` 的出队为何要用 `OneHotInOrder` 断言？
**答案**：CoralNPU 是 in-order 核，派发必须按程序顺序进行。该断言保证「只有当队首指令被消费后，才能消费第二条」，防止后端的乱序 ready 信号把指令打乱。

**练习 2**：缓冲容量为什么定成 `fetchInstrSlots * 2 = 16`？
**答案**：一行 fetch 来 8 条、译码每周期吃 4 条。深度 16（两行的量）足以吸收「一次突发 8 条」与「派发端偶尔反压」之间的短期失配，又不至于过度占用面积。
